name: Account Equity Snapshot
description: Provides a real-time summary of the account's total value, combining cash and security holdings.
usage: Used when a user asks for account equity, net worth, net liquidation value, or total account value.

name: Account Buying Power
description: Calculates the total capital available for new trade purchases based on account type (cash or margin) and current leverage.
usage: Used when a user asks how much stock they can buy, what is their buying power, or available funds for trading.

name: Account Cash Availability
description: Breaks down the cash balance by settlement status and availability for withdrawal.
usage: Used to determine specific liquidity constraints, settled vs unsettled funds, and cash available for withdrawal.

name: Pending Cash Commitments
description: Identifies cash currently reserved in open orders that has not yet been executed, reducing the actual amount available for new trades.
usage: Used to determine the true tradable cash by subtracting funds locked in working orders from the total cash balance. Essential for preventing overdrafts or good faith violations.

name: Cash Sweep & Yield Details
description: Provides specifics on where uninvested cash is held (e.g., money market fund, bank deposit) and the current interest rate/yield being earned on that cash.
usage: Used when a user asks about their idle cash, interest income, or where their cash is parked while not invested.

name: Pattern Day Trader (PDT) Status
description: Identifies if the account is flagged as a Pattern Day Trader, tracks the number of day trades used in the rolling 5-day window, and confirms if the minimum equity requirement is met.
usage: Used to determine eligibility for day-trading buying power, check how many day trades are left before a restriction, or understand why leverage might be unavailable.

name: Margin Loan & Interest Summary
description: Displays the current debit balance (amount borrowed on margin) and the associated interest rates, which directly reduce the account's equity and available buying power.
usage: Used when calculating the cost of leverage, determining how much debt is eating into buying power, or checking interest charges.

name: Account Trading Status
description: Indicates the operational state of the account to determine if order entry is permitted or if the account is frozen/closed.
usage: Used when a user asks if their account is locked, frozen, closed, or active for trading. It provides the high-level 'go/no-go' status for the account.

name: Account Restriction Flags
description: Lists specific active compliance or administrative flags that limit trading capabilities (e.g., no opening transactions, settlement holds, good faith violations).
usage: Used to identify specific reasons why trading might be blocked or limited, such as a hard block on new purchases or a restriction on specific securities.

name: Day Trade Transaction Log
description: Provides a granular list of specific executions classified as day trades within the rolling 5-day window, including symbol, open time, and close time.
usage: Used when a user needs to verify which specific trades contributed to the PDT count, audit the flag's accuracy, or understand exactly what constitutes a day trade in their account.

name: PDT Reset Timeline
description: Calculates the precise dates and times when current day trades will expire from the rolling 5-day window, projecting when the account will revert to a non-flagged status or when buying power will be restored.
usage: Used to determine exactly when a day trade will 'fall off' the count, helping the user plan future trading activity to avoid restrictions or regain eligibility.

name: Open Positions Market Value Breakdown
description: Provides a detailed list of all currently held securities, including quantities, current prices, and the individual market value that sums up to the total portfolio value.
usage: Used when a user asks to see what specific stocks or assets they own, the value of each position, or to verify the composition of their total portfolio market value.

name: Unrealized P&L Summary
description: Calculates the total profit or loss for the portfolio based on the current market value compared to the original cost basis of all open positions.
usage: Used when a user asks how much money they have made or lost on their current holdings, or wants to see the performance of their portfolio's market value relative to what they paid.

name: Sector Allocation Breakdown
description: Aggregates the total market value of holdings by industry sector to show how the portfolio's value is distributed across different market segments.
usage: Used when a user asks what industries their portfolio is exposed to, or wants to see the concentration of market value by sector (e.g., Tech, Healthcare).

name: Position Level Unrealized P&L
description: Calculates the unrealized gain or loss for each individual open position, allowing for a granular view of performance at the security level.
usage: Used when a user wants to see which specific stocks or assets are driving their total portfolio profit or loss, or to analyze the performance of individual holdings relative to their cost basis.

name: Sector Unrealized P&L Breakdown
description: Aggregates the total unrealized profit and loss by industry sector to show which specific areas of the market are contributing to gains or losses.
usage: Used when a user wants to understand if their total unrealized P&L is concentrated in specific industries (e.g., 'Tech is dragging me down' or 'Energy is driving my gains').

name: Unrealized P&L Historical Trend
description: Tracks the total unrealized P&L of the portfolio over a specified historical timeframe (e.g., 1M, 3M, YTD) to show how paper gains/losses have evolved.
usage: Used when a user asks how their unrealized P&L has changed over time, or wants to visualize the trajectory of their open positions' performance.

name: Daily Realized P&L Summary
description: Calculates the total net profit or loss generated from closed positions and completed trades within the current trading day.
usage: Used when a user asks for their daily realized P&L, how much they made on closed trades today, or net profit from trading activity today.

name: Realized P&L by Symbol
description: Breaks down the daily realized profit and loss by individual ticker, showing which specific securities contributed to gains or losses.
usage: Used to identify winning and losing stocks for the day, or to see detailed P&L data per asset for today's trading session.

name: Position Counts Summary
description: Provides a numerical summary of the account's open positions, aggregating the total count and breaking it down by side (long/short) and asset class.
usage: Used when a user asks how many positions they have, how many stocks they own, or wants a quick count of their holdings without fetching the full market value list.

name: Top Holdings by Market Value
description: Identifies and ranks the open positions with the highest market values in descending order, providing a focused view of the largest assets in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user asks for their largest holdings, biggest positions, or specifically wants to know which open position has the largest market value.

name: Smallest Holdings by Market Value
description: Identifies and ranks the open positions with the lowest market values in ascending order, providing a focused view of the smallest assets in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user asks for their smallest holdings, least valuable positions, or specifically wants to know which open position has the smallest market value. Useful for identifying 'dust' or consolidating small lots.

name: Top Positions by Unrealized Gain
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their unrealized profit, sorted by either absolute dollar amount or percentage gain to highlight the portfolio's best performers.
usage: Used when a user asks which position is the biggest winner, what is the highest unrealized gain, or what are the top performing stocks currently held.

name: Top Positions by Unrealized Loss
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their negative unrealized profit, sorted by either absolute dollar loss or percentage loss to highlight the portfolio's worst performers.
usage: Used when a user asks which position is the biggest loser, what is the largest unrealized loss, or what are the worst performing stocks currently held.

name: Gross vs Net Market Value Analysis
description: Calculates the gross exposure (sum of long and absolute short market values) and net exposure (difference between long and short) to determine total leverage and directional bias.
usage: Used when a user asks for their net long vs short exposure, total market exposure, or wants to understand their portfolio's leverage relative to their net worth.

name: Sector Long/Short Exposure Breakdown
description: Breaks down market value by industry sector, separating long and short positions to show gross exposure and net exposure within each specific market segment.
usage: Used to determine which specific industries the user is net long or net short in, and to identify concentration of long vs short bets by sector.

name: Beta-Weighted Portfolio Exposure
description: Calculates the portfolio's aggregate delta exposure relative to a benchmark index (e.g., SPY) using individual position betas, providing a view of systematic risk exposure.
usage: Used when a user asks for their portfolio's beta, how much market risk they are exposed to, or if their book is market-neutral.

name: Asset Class Allocation Breakdown
description: Aggregates the total market value of the portfolio by broad asset class (e.g., Equities, Fixed Income, Cash, Derivatives) to show capital deployment and diversification.
usage: Used when a user asks for the percentage of the portfolio in cash, stocks, or bonds, or wants to understand their high-level asset mix and how much capital is deployed versus idle.

name: Margin Usage & Utilization Rate
description: Calculates the percentage of margin currently utilized relative to the account's maintenance margin requirement, showing how close the account is to a margin call.
usage: Used when a user asks for current margin usage, how much of their margin is used, or how close they are to a margin call.

name: Margin Multiplier & Leverage Settings
description: Identifies the specific leverage multiples applicable to the account based on its type and settlement status (e.g., 2:1 for Reg T overnight, 4:1 for Day Trading).
usage: Used when a user asks what their margin multiplier is, what leverage they are allowed to use, or to distinguish between intraday and overnight leverage limits.

name: Position Cost Basis Breakdown
description: Returns the total cost basis and average cost per share for every open position in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user asks for the original purchase price of their holdings, average price paid per share, or needs the cost basis value to calculate returns independently or for tax reporting.

name: Equity Instrument Type Allocation
description: Aggregates the market value of equity holdings by the specific classification of the security instrument, distinguishing between Common Stock, ETFs, ADRs, Preferred Stock, and REITs.
usage: Used when a user asks for the breakdown of their equity portfolio between individual stocks and funds, or wants to see the concentration of specific financial instruments.

name: Market Capitalization Exposure Breakdown
description: Categorizes equity positions into market cap buckets (Mega, Large, Mid, Small, Micro) based on the issuer's total market valuation.
usage: Used when a user wants to see the size distribution of their equity holdings, determine if they are heavy on small-caps, or analyze exposure to different company sizes.

name: Geographic Equity Exposure
description: Aggregates equity market value by the region or country of domicile for the underlying issuer (e.g., US, Developed International, Emerging Markets, Specific Countries).
usage: Used when a user asks how much of their equity portfolio is invested in US stocks versus international stocks, or wants to analyze home bias and global diversification.

name: Net Buying Power
description: Calculates the actual buying power available for new orders by subtracting the estimated total cost and margin requirements of all currently open working orders from the account's total gross buying power.
usage: Used when a user asks for their available buying power, how much they can actually spend right now, or specifically wants to know their buying power adjusted for pending commitments.

name: Open Orders List
description: Retrieves a detailed list of all currently active, working, or pending orders that have not yet been filled, canceled, or expired.
usage: Used when a user asks what orders are in the queue, do I have any open orders, or wants to review/edit pending buy/sell instructions.

name: Open Order Type Breakdown
description: Categorizes all active working orders by their specific execution type (Market, Limit, Stop, Stop-Limit), providing the parameters that distinguish them.
usage: Used when a user needs to distinguish specifically which orders are market orders versus limit orders, or to analyze the execution logic of pending orders.

name: Market Order Liquidity Exposure
description: Calculates the total notional value of all open market orders to quantify immediate execution risk and capital commitment.
usage: Used to assess how much capital is currently committed to immediate fills at market prices versus limit prices, helping to gauge slippage risk and cash requirements.

name: Stop Orders List
description: Retrieves a filtered list of all currently active orders that are conditional, specifically Stop and Stop-Limit orders, excluding simple Market or Limit orders.
usage: Used when a user asks for their stop losses, open stop orders, or specifically wants to see orders that will trigger on price movement.

name: Stop Order Trigger Proximity
description: Calculates the percentage and absolute distance between the current market price and the stop trigger price for all open Stop and Stop-Limit orders.
usage: Used to assess how close a stop order is to being executed, or to determine if stops are set too tight or too loose relative to current volatility.

name: Stop Order Aggregate Exposure
description: Calculates the total notional value (Quantity x Stop Price) of all open Stop and Stop-Limit orders to understand potential capital outflows or inflows if triggered.
usage: Used to gauge the potential liquidity impact or market impact if all stop orders were to execute simultaneously at their trigger prices.

name: Bracket Order Group Summary
description: Identifies and groups orders that form a complete bracket strategy, consisting of a primary entry order, a stop-loss order, and a take-profit limit order.
usage: Used when a user asks to find all active bracket orders, review attached risk management legs, or verify that a stop and target are properly linked to a primary order.

name: Order Linkage & Contingency Map
description: Maps the parent-child relationships between orders, showing which contingent orders (stops, targets, trailing stops) are attached to specific parent orders.
usage: Used to understand the dependency structure of complex orders, identify which orders will cancel others (OCO), or verify that risk management orders are attached to the correct parent.

name: Day-Only Orders List
description: Filters the open order book to display only orders with a 'Day' Time in Force (TIF) setting, which will be automatically canceled if not filled by the market close.
usage: Used when a user asks about orders expiring today, day-only orders, or wants to identify temporary orders that will not carry over to the next session.

name: Time in Force (TIF) Breakdown
description: Categorizes all open orders by their Time in Force attribute (Day, GTC, GTD, IOC, FOK) to show the distribution of order lifecycles in the queue.
usage: Used to analyze the persistence of pending orders, distinguishing between intraday orders and those meant to last days or weeks.

name: Order Expiration Schedule
description: Lists the specific date and time when each open order is set to expire or be canceled, regardless of its Time in Force type.
usage: Used to review upcoming cancellations (e.g., Good-Til-Date orders) or to confirm when Day orders will automatically terminate at the session close.

name: Partially Filled Orders Breakdown
description: Identifies and filters open orders where execution has occurred but the total quantity has not yet been filled, distinguishing active working orders from untouched orders.
usage: Used to identify orders currently in the process of being filled, track remaining quantity, and monitor the average entry price of partial fills during the trading session.

name: Daily Order Execution Log
description: Provides a chronological record of all individual fill events (executions) that occurred during the current trading day, linked to their parent orders.
usage: Used to audit specific trade activity, verify exact fill prices and timestamps, and determine which specific executions caused orders to become partially filled.

name: Daily Filled Orders List
description: Aggregates individual execution legs to display a list of orders that reached a fully filled status during the current trading day, providing a summary of completed transactions.
usage: Used when a user asks which orders were completed today, what finished executing, or requests a record of closed orders for the session.

name: Order Fill Quality Analysis
description: Evaluates the execution efficiency of filled orders by comparing the average fill price against the order's limit price or the midpoint at entry to calculate slippage or price improvement.
usage: Used to assess how well orders were executed, identify unfavorable fills, or calculate the cost of implementation for the day's trades.

name: Daily Trade Activity Summary
description: Provides high-level metrics for the current trading session, aggregating data from all filled orders to show total volume, turnover, and counts.
usage: Used when a user asks how much they traded today, what the total turnover was, or wants a snapshot of daily trading activity.

name: Daily Cancelled Orders List
description: Lists all orders that had their status changed to 'Cancelled' during the current trading session, including manual cancellations and system terminations.
usage: Used when a user asks for a history of cancelled orders, wants to audit order management, or needs to see which specific orders were pulled from the queue.

name: Expired Orders Log
description: Identifies orders that were automatically terminated by the system because they reached their Time-in-Force (TIF) expiration without being filled (e.g., Day orders at market close, GTD orders).
usage: Used to distinguish between user-initiated cancellations and system expirations, helping users understand why orders disappeared without manual intervention.

name: Order Rejection Log
description: Lists orders that were rejected by the broker or exchange immediately upon submission and were never activated or worked.
usage: Used to troubleshoot failed order attempts, identify insufficient funds errors, or understand why a specific order request was denied by the system.

name: Partially Filled & Cancelled Orders
description: Filters cancelled orders to show only those that had partial executions before being terminated, highlighting liquidity that was obtained versus missed.
usage: Used when analyzing fill rates, determining how much of an intended position was actually acquired before cancellation, or reviewing slippage on incomplete orders.

name: Open Orders Notional Value Summary
description: Calculates the aggregate monetary value of all currently active orders by summing the quantity multiplied by the price (limit price or estimated market price) for every order type.
usage: Used when a user asks for the total notional value of open orders, the gross exposure of pending trades, or the combined value of all orders currently in the queue.

name: Daily Win/Loss Trade Count
description: Categorizes all completed trades for the current day into 'Winning' and 'Losing' buckets based on realized P&L, providing the specific count and percentage for each.
usage: Used when a user asks for their batting average, hit rate, or specifically wants to know the number of profitable trades versus unprofitable trades executed today.

name: Intraday Trade Frequency Profile
description: Distributes the total count of executed trades across the trading session by time intervals (e.g., hourly or 30-minute bars) to show the velocity of trading activity.
usage: Used to identify peaks and lulls in activity, determine how many trades occurred in the morning versus the afternoon, or analyze market timing patterns.

name: Order vs Fill Granularity Comparison
description: Distinguishes between the total number of distinct order tickets filled and the total number of individual execution legs (partial fills) processed by the exchange.
usage: Used to understand execution complexity, specifically to see how many orders resulted in partial fills versus single blocks, differentiating 'tickets' from 'transactions'.

name: Rolling 5-Day Day Trade Distribution
description: Aggregates the total number of day trades executed on each specific trading day within the current rolling 5-day window.
usage: Used when a user wants to see a breakdown of their day trade activity by date (e.g., 'Did I trade on Monday?'), rather than just a total sum. It answers the specific request for a count 'over the last five trading days' by showing the distribution.

name: Day Trade Count by Symbol
description: Aggregates the total number of day trades executed for each specific ticker within the rolling 5-day window.
usage: Used to identify which specific securities are contributing most to the day trade count, helping users manage their limit by avoiding high-frequency symbols.

name: Day Trade Limit Status
description: Calculates the specific integer values for trades used, trades remaining, and the total limit based on account type and equity status.
usage: Used when a user asks 'how many day trades do I have left' or needs a quick numeric reference of their current standing relative to the PDT rule threshold (e.g., 3/3).

name: Historical Realized P&L Summary
description: Calculates the total net profit or loss generated from all closed positions over a specified historical date range (e.g., last 30 days, YTD, 1 year).
usage: Used when a user asks for total realized gains/losses over a specific past period, monthly performance, or wants to know how much they made on closed trades excluding the current session.

name: Historical Realized P&L by Symbol
description: Breaks down the total realized profit or loss by individual ticker within a specified historical date range to identify the best and worst performing assets.
usage: Used to determine which specific stocks or assets contributed to gains or losses over a past period (e.g., 'Which stocks made me money last month?').

name: Realized P&L Daily Time Series
description: Provides a chronological series of daily realized P&L values over a specified historical period, allowing for analysis of consistency and volatility in trading returns.
usage: Used when a user wants to see their performance day-by-day over the last month, identify profitable versus unprofitable trading days, or view the trend of their realized returns.

name: Dividend Transaction History
description: Retrieves a chronological record of all dividend and capital gain distribution payments credited to the account, including the associated security and payment date.
usage: Used when a user asks for dividend payments received, income history, or a specific list of dividends paid within a date range (e.g., last 30 days).

name: Total Dividend Income Summary
description: Aggregates the total monetary value of all dividend payments received over a specific period, providing a single sum of income generated from holdings.
usage: Used when a user asks for the total amount of dividends received, how much income was generated in the last month, or the sum of all dividend payments.

name: Portfolio Dividend Yield
description: Calculates the weighted average dividend yield of the entire portfolio based on current market values and the annualized dividend rate of individual holdings.
usage: Used when a user asks for the overall dividend yield of their portfolio, the income return percentage, or how efficient their holdings are at generating cash flow relative to value.

name: Monthly Dividend Income Breakdown
description: Aggregates total dividend payments received by month (e.g., January, February, March) over the current calendar year to show income distribution.
usage: Used when a user wants to analyze the consistency of their income stream or see which months generated the most cash flow within the year-to-date period.

name: Dividend Income by Sector
description: Categorizes and aggregates year-to-date dividend payments by the industry sector of the issuing security to identify income concentration.
usage: Used to determine which sectors (e.g., Energy, Tech, Utilities) are contributing the most to the user's YTD dividend income.

name: Fee & Commission Transaction History
description: Retrieves a detailed, itemized list of all fees, commissions, and other transaction costs charged to the account within a specified date range.
usage: Used when a user needs to see a specific list of fees charged, audit transaction costs, or identify individual regulatory or brokerage charges over a period.

name: Total Fees & Commissions Summary
description: Aggregates the total monetary value of all fees and commissions incurred over a specific period, with optional breakdowns by fee category (e.g., commissions, regulatory fees, exchange fees).
usage: Used when a user asks for the total cost of trading over a month, wants to calculate net returns including explicit costs, or analyze the impact of fees on portfolio performance.

name: Cash Transfers History
description: Provides a chronological record of all external cash movements into and out of the account, including deposits, withdrawals, and electronic fund transfers.
usage: Used when a user asks for their deposit/withdrawal history, funding history, or needs to track external money flows to calculate net cash flow.

name: Interest Income History
description: Lists all interest payments credited to the account from cash holdings (e.g., money market funds, bank deposits, credit interest) over a specific period.
usage: Used when a user asks how much interest they earned, wants to verify yield payments, or needs to include interest income in a total cash flow calculation.

name: Trading Cash Flow Summary
description: Aggregates the total cash outflow from buy transactions and total cash inflow from sell transactions, distinct from realized P&L, to show the liquidity impact of trading.
usage: Used to determine how much cash was consumed by buying securities or generated by selling them, which is a key component of net cash flow alongside P&L and transfers.

name: Unrealized P&L Percentage Filter
description: Filters the list of open positions to return only those where the unrealized gain or loss percentage meets a specific threshold criteria (e.g., loss greater than 5%).
usage: Used when a user asks for positions losing more than X%, positions gaining more than Y%, or needs to isolate holdings based on a specific performance percentage range.

name: Tax Lot Level P&L Analysis
description: Breaks down the unrealized profit or loss by individual tax lot within a position, rather than providing the aggregate position-level average.
usage: Used to identify specific tax lots that are in a loss position for tax-loss harvesting, or to determine which specific purchase tranches are underwater versus profitable.

name: Sector Breakdown of Top Performing Positions
description: Aggregates the market value and unrealized P&L of positions that meet a specific performance criteria (e.g., >10% gain) by their industry sector.
usage: Used when a user wants to know which industries are driving their biggest winning positions, or to see the sector concentration of their top gainers.

name: Technical Overextension Analysis for Top Gainers
description: Calculates technical momentum indicators (RSI, distance from moving averages, Bollinger Band position) specifically for positions where the unrealized gain exceeds a defined threshold.
usage: Used to determine if positions with large gains are overbought, extended from their mean, or showing signs of exhaustion to inform sell/hold decisions.

name: 52-Week High Proximity Filter
description: Identifies open positions where the current market price is within a specified percentage threshold of the security's 52-week high.
usage: Used when a user asks for stocks at their highs, which positions are breaking out, or lists securities near their peak price over the last year.

name: 52-Week Low Proximity Filter
description: Identifies open positions where the current market price is within a specified percentage threshold of the security's 52-week low.
usage: Used when a user asks for stocks at their lows, which positions are hitting support, or lists securities near their lowest price over the last year.

name: Position 52-Week Range Status
description: Calculates the percentile ranking of the current price within the 52-week trading range for every open position, showing where each security stands relative to its yearly high and low.
usage: Used to get a high-level view of portfolio strength by seeing which holdings are in the top or bottom quartile of their yearly price range without applying a specific filter.

name: Distance to 52-Week Low Analysis
description: Calculates the specific percentage difference between the current market price and the 52-week low for every open position in the portfolio.
usage: Used to view the exact drawdown percentage for all holdings to identify how close they are to yearly lows without applying a hard filter. Useful for ranking positions by their proximity to the bottom or identifying borderline cases.

name: 52-Week Low Date Reference
description: Provides the specific calendar date on which the 52-week low was established for each open position.
usage: Used to add temporal context to price levels, helping users determine if a position is near a low that was established recently (potential fresh breakdown) or one that occurred months ago (potential recovery zone).

name: Position 52-Week High & Low Prices
description: Returns the specific highest and lowest price points reached over the trailing 52-week period for every open position in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user explicitly asks for the 52-week high and low values of their holdings, or needs to reference these exact price levels to determine support/resistance boundaries.

name: Open Position Volume & Liquidity Summary
description: Provides comprehensive trading volume statistics for all currently open positions, including intraday volume, historical averages, and public float data.
usage: Used when a user asks for the average daily volume of their holdings, wants to assess the liquidity of a specific position, or needs to compare current trading activity to historical norms.

name: Low Liquidity Position Filter
description: Identifies open positions where the average daily volume falls below a user-defined threshold, flagging securities that may be difficult to exit quickly or without significant slippage.
usage: Used when a user wants to find illiquid stocks in their portfolio, assess liquidity risk, or filter for positions that trade less than a specific number of shares per day.

name: Position Level Realized Volatility
description: Calculates the annualized standard deviation of daily returns for each open position over a specific lookback period (e.g., 20 or 30 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks for the volatility of specific holdings, wants to compare risk across positions, or needs to determine which stocks are moving the most.

name: Realized Volatility Historical Percentile
description: Determines the percentile rank of the current realized volatility relative to the stock's own volatility history over the past year (e.g., 252 trading days).
usage: Used to assess if a position's current price movement is statistically normal or if the stock is entering a high-volatility regime compared to its past behavior.

name: Historical Volatility Term Structure
description: Provides realized volatility metrics across multiple lookback periods (e.g., 10-day, 30-day, 60-day, 90-day) to visualize the trend of volatility over time.
usage: Used to identify if volatility is expanding (short-term vol > long-term vol) or contracting, helping to predict potential breakouts or mean reversion.

name: Implied vs Realized Volatility Spread
description: Compares the calculated realized volatility of the stock against the market's implied volatility (derived from options pricing) to show the premium or discount the market is placing on future moves.
usage: Used when a user wants to know if a stock is 'expensive' or 'cheap' in terms of volatility expectations, or to assess the likelihood of an earnings surprise.

name: Position Level Beta vs Benchmark
description: Calculates the beta coefficient for each open position relative to a specified benchmark index (e.g., SPY) over a defined lookback period (e.g., 60 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks for the beta of individual stocks they own, wants to analyze the systematic risk of their holdings against the market, or requests beta for a specific timeframe.

name: Moving Average Crossover Status
description: Determines the relationship between a short-term moving average (e.g., 50-day) and a long-term moving average (e.g., 200-day) for each open position, indicating if the trend is bullish (above) or bearish (below).
usage: Used when a user asks if the 50-day is above the 200-day, wants to identify Golden Crosses or Death Crosses in their portfolio, or needs to check the long-term trend alignment of their holdings.

name: Position Moving Averages
description: Provides the specific numerical values for Simple Moving Averages (SMA) over defined periods for each open position in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user needs to see the actual price level of the 50-day or 200-day line, wants to perform custom calculations on moving averages, or verify the exact values used in crossover calculations.

name: Price vs Moving Average Proximity
description: Calculates the percentage distance between the current market price and specific moving averages (e.g., 50-day, 200-day) for all open positions.
usage: Used when a user asks if the current stock price is trading above or below a key moving average, wants to identify pullbacks to the mean, or needs to see how extended a price is from its 50/200-day support levels.

name: Position Level 14-Day RSI
description: Calculates the 14-day Relative Strength Index (RSI) for every open position in the portfolio to identify momentum strength and potential overbought or oversold conditions.
usage: Used when a user asks for the RSI of their holdings, wants to check if stocks are overbought (above 70) or oversold (below 30), or specifically requests the 14-period RSI for positions.

name: Position Level 14-Day Average True Range
description: Calculates the 14-period Average True Range (ATR) for every open position in the portfolio to measure volatility and potential daily price movement, accounting for gaps and limit moves.
usage: Used when a user asks for the 14-day ATR of their holdings, wants to gauge the average trading range, or needs this metric to calculate volatility-based stop-loss levels.

name: Position Daily Price Change
description: Provides the current trading price, previous session close, and the resulting daily percentage and absolute change for all open positions.
usage: Used when a user asks for today's percent change, how much a specific stock moved today, or the current session's performance for their holdings.

name: Portfolio Daily Return Contribution by Position
description: Calculates the specific contribution of each open position to the total account's daily return by weighting the position's daily percentage change by its market value weight.
usage: Used to determine which specific holdings drove the overall account performance for the day, identifying the heaviest weights in the portfolio's intraday move.

name: Sector Daily Performance Breakdown
description: Aggregates the daily price performance of open positions by industry sector to show which market segments are contributing positively or negatively to the portfolio's daily value change.
usage: Used when a user wants to see which sectors are up or down today based on their specific holdings, or needs to understand the sector attribution of daily P&L.

name: Position Intraday P&L
description: Calculates the profit or loss for each open position specifically for the current trading session, based on the difference between the current market price and the previous session's closing price multiplied by the quantity held.
usage: Used when a user asks how much a specific position is up or down today in dollar terms, distinct from the total cost basis or lifetime unrealized P&L.

name: Top Open Positions by Intraday Gain
description: Identifies and ranks the open positions with the highest positive performance for the current trading session, sorted by intraday P&L.
usage: Used when a user wants to see which positions are carrying the portfolio today, or asks 'what are my best performers right now'.

name: Worst Open Positions by Intraday Loss
description: Identifies and ranks the open positions with the lowest negative performance for the current trading session, sorted by intraday P&L.
usage: Used when a user wants to see which positions are dragging the portfolio down today, or asks 'what is hurting my P&L right now'.

name: Average Daily Dollar Volume Ranking
description: Calculates the average daily dollar volume (Average Price x Average Volume) for each open position over a specified period and ranks them from highest to lowest to identify liquidity in terms of capital flow.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions trade the most money, which stocks have the highest liquidity in dollar terms, or needs to identify where large orders might face the least slippage.

name: Lowest Average Daily Dollar Volume Ranking
description: Ranks open positions by their average daily dollar volume (Price x Volume) in ascending order, highlighting the assets with the least liquidity in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user asks for the stocks with the lowest trading volume, wants to identify liquidity traps, or specifically requests a bottom-up view of market activity.

name: 30-Day Average Daily Dollar Volume
description: Calculates the specific 30-trading-day average of the daily dollar volume for each open position to assess short-term liquidity trends.
usage: Used when a user specifically asks for liquidity metrics over the last month or needs to compare positions based on a strict 30-day lookback window.

name: Sector Position Count Breakdown
description: Allocates the total number of distinct open positions by industry sector, showing diversification in terms of the count of securities held rather than dollar value.
usage: Used when a user wants to know how many different stocks they own in each sector to assess name concentration or breadth, independent of the capital allocated.

name: Sector Cost Basis Allocation
description: Aggregates the original investment cost (cost basis) of holdings by industry sector to show where capital was originally deployed, distinct from current market value allocation.
usage: Used when a user wants to see their historical capital allocation by sector or compare initial investment weight to current market value weight to identify valuation drift.

name: Sector Over/Underweight Analysis
description: Compares the portfolio's sector weightings (percentage of total market value) against a benchmark index's sector weights (e.g., S&P 500) to identify active bets.
usage: Used when a user asks if they are too heavy in a specific sector compared to the market, wants to identify overweights/underweights, or needs to see their active sector tilts.

name: Revenue-Based Sector Exposure
description: Reclassifies portfolio allocation weights based on the actual source of revenue for the underlying companies (e.g., a 'Consumer Discretionary' stock might generate 50% revenue from 'Technology' services), providing an economic view of exposure rather than just a classification view.
usage: Used when a user wants to understand their 'true' economic exposure or where their companies actually make their money, which differs from the standard sector classification of the ticker.

name: Thematic Allocation Breakdown
description: Aggregates portfolio holdings by investment themes (e.g., Clean Energy, Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, ESG) that often cut across traditional industry sector boundaries.
usage: Used when a user wants to see exposure to specific narratives, macro trends, or megatrends rather than standard GICS sectors.

name: Historical Industry Allocation Time Series
description: Tracks the percentage allocation of capital by industry or sector over a historical timeframe (e.g., monthly snapshots) to visualize how portfolio composition has drifted due to market performance.
usage: Used to analyze how concentration has changed over time, identify if a winning sector has become an outsized portion of the portfolio (drift), or review the history of diversification changes.

name: Top Sectors by Portfolio Weight
description: Ranks industry sectors by their percentage contribution to the total portfolio market value in descending order, highlighting the largest exposures and concentration risks.
usage: Used when a user asks for the biggest sectors, largest weights, or which industries dominate their portfolio composition.

name: Smallest Sectors by Portfolio Weight
description: Ranks industry sectors by their percentage contribution to the total portfolio market value in ascending order, highlighting the smallest exposures and potential areas for diversification.
usage: Used when a user asks for the smallest sectors, least exposed industries, or which sectors have minimal allocation.

name: Position Volatility Contribution Breakdown
description: Calculates the specific contribution of each open position to the total portfolio volatility (variance), accounting for the position's weight, individual volatility, and correlation with other holdings.
usage: Used when a user asks which specific stocks are driving the portfolio's overall risk or volatility, or wants to identify the primary sources of variance in the portfolio.

name: Portfolio Correlation Matrix
description: Computes the pairwise correlation coefficients between all open positions in the portfolio over a specified timeframe.
usage: Used to understand the diversification benefits or risks within the portfolio, identifying which assets move together and which move inversely.

name: Position Idiosyncratic vs. Systematic Risk
description: Decomposes the total risk (variance) of each position into systematic risk (market-driven) and idiosyncratic risk (asset-specific), typically using regression against a benchmark.
usage: Used to determine if a position's volatility is due to broad market movements or specific company factors, aiding in risk management and hedging decisions.

name: Top Holdings Correlation Matrix
description: Computes the pairwise correlation coefficients specifically for the portfolio's top N positions (e.g., top 5, 10) ranked by market value, rather than the entire universe of open positions.
usage: Used when a user wants to assess the diversification or concentration risk of their largest investments, or specifically asks 'how correlated are my biggest positions?' without needing the noise of smaller holdings.

name: Top Holdings Historical Return Series
description: Provides the daily percentage price change (returns) time series for the portfolio's top N positions over a specified historical period.
usage: Used to retrieve the underlying data needed to calculate correlation, volatility, or beta for the largest holdings, or to visualize the performance trends of the primary portfolio drivers.

name: Correlation Statistical Significance Analysis
description: Calculates the p-values and confidence intervals for the correlation coefficients between holdings to determine if the relationships are statistically robust or likely due to random chance.
usage: Used to validate correlation metrics, especially over shorter timeframes (e.g., 60 days) where data may be noisy, helping the user distinguish real dependency from spurious correlation.

name: Top Correlated Position Pairs
description: Calculates the correlation coefficient for every unique pair of open positions over a specified lookback period (e.g., 60 days) and ranks them to identify the pairs with the highest positive correlation.
usage: Used when a user asks 'Which two positions are most positively correlated?', 'Which stocks move together the most?', or needs to identify redundant exposure and concentration risk within the portfolio.

name: Most Negatively Correlated Position Pairs
description: Calculates the correlation coefficient for every unique pair of open positions over a specified lookback period and ranks them to identify the pairs with the lowest (most negative) correlation.
usage: Used when a user wants to identify diversification benefits, find natural hedges, or determine which positions move inversely to one another to offset portfolio volatility.

name: All Holdings Historical Return Series
description: Provides the daily percentage price change (returns) time series for *all* open positions in the portfolio over a specified historical period, not just the top holdings.
usage: Used to retrieve the underlying data required to calculate correlation matrices for the entire portfolio universe, ensuring that smaller positions are included in the analysis.

name: Position Pair Rolling Correlation
description: Calculates the rolling correlation coefficient between position pairs over a specified window (e.g., 20-day rolling) to visualize how the relationship has evolved over time.
usage: Used to determine if a negative correlation identified in a 60-day snapshot is a stable trend or a recent anomaly, helping to assess the reliability of the hedge.

name: Correlation Stability Analysis
description: Calculates the standard deviation of the correlation coefficient for position pairs over the specified 60-day period to quantify the volatility or 'noise' within the relationship.
usage: Used to assess the reliability of the correlation metric; a high standard deviation indicates that the relationship is fluctuating significantly, making the 'most negatively correlated' status potentially unstable.

name: Portfolio Sharpe Ratio
description: Calculates the annualized Sharpe ratio for the portfolio over a specified timeframe (e.g., YTD) using daily total returns and a risk-free rate benchmark.
usage: Used when a user asks for the portfolio's risk-adjusted return, Sharpe ratio, or how much return the portfolio generates per unit of risk.

name: Portfolio Daily Total Return Series
description: Computes the daily percentage return for the total portfolio value (equity + cash) over a historical period, providing the raw data needed for risk analytics.
usage: Used as the foundational input for calculating risk metrics like Sharpe, Sortino, or volatility, where daily percentage changes are required rather than absolute P&L values.

name: Portfolio Aggregate Volatility
description: Calculates the annualized standard deviation of the portfolio's daily total returns over a specific lookback period, representing the overall risk profile of the aggregated holdings.
usage: Used when a user asks for the total portfolio volatility or standard deviation. It serves as the denominator in the Sharpe ratio calculation and is distinct from individual position volatility.

name: Portfolio Max Drawdown
description: Identifies the largest peak-to-trough decline in total portfolio value over a specified period, expressed as a percentage, to measure downside risk.
usage: Used to assess the worst-case historical loss scenario for the portfolio, often viewed alongside Sharpe ratio to determine if the risk-adjusted returns justify the potential drawdowns.

name: Portfolio Sortino Ratio
description: Calculates the annualized Sortino ratio for the portfolio, which measures risk-adjusted return by penalizing only for downside volatility (returns falling below a minimum acceptable return) rather than total volatility.
usage: Used when a user asks for the Sortino ratio, or wants to evaluate portfolio performance focusing specifically on downside protection and the volatility of bad outcomes.

name: Downside Deviation Calculation
description: Computes the standard deviation of negative asset returns (returns falling below a specified target threshold), representing the 'bad' volatility of the portfolio.
usage: Used to isolate the risk of loss from overall volatility, essential for calculating the Sortino ratio, or assessing the portfolio's specific tendency to decline relative to a benchmark.

name: Rolling Portfolio Volatility Series
description: Calculates the annualized standard deviation of daily portfolio returns over a specified rolling window (e.g., 30 days) across a historical timeframe.
usage: Used to visualize the trend of portfolio risk over time, specifically answering how volatility has evolved during the last 6 months and identifying periods of expansion or contraction.

name: Rolling Benchmark Volatility Series
description: Calculates the annualized standard deviation of daily returns for a specified benchmark index (e.g., SPY) over a rolling window and historical period.
usage: Used to compare portfolio volatility against market volatility to determine if portfolio changes are due to systematic market risk or idiosyncratic factors.

name: Rolling Value at Risk (VaR) Series
description: Calculates the maximum potential loss with a given confidence interval (e.g., 95%) over a rolling window of historical portfolio returns.
usage: Used to track how the absolute downside risk (in dollars or percentage) has fluctuated over time, complementing the volatility analysis with a view of potential loss magnitude.

name: Benchmark Historical Return Summary
description: Calculates the total percentage return, price change, and volatility of a specific market benchmark (e.g., SPY, QQQ) over a defined historical period.
usage: Used when a user wants to know the market's performance over a specific timeframe to compare against their portfolio or to gauge general market conditions.

name: Portfolio vs Benchmark Performance Comparison
description: Calculates the portfolio's return alongside the benchmark's return over a specific period, determining the excess return (alpha) and relative strength.
usage: Used to answer 'Did I beat the market?', 'What is my excess return?', or to compare portfolio performance directly against an index.

name: Tracking Error Analysis
description: Calculates the standard deviation of the difference between the portfolio's returns and the benchmark's returns, quantifying how closely the portfolio tracks the index.
usage: Used to measure the riskiness of active management, or to determine how much the portfolio's performance deviates from the benchmark on a day-to-day basis.

name: Daily Return Differential Series
description: Calculates the day-by-day difference between the portfolio's daily return and the benchmark's daily return over a specified period.
usage: Used to identify specific days where portfolio performance deviated significantly from the benchmark, helping to explain the sources of volatility contributing to the tracking error metric.

name: Rolling Tracking Error Series
description: Calculates the tracking error (standard deviation of return differences) over a rolling window (e.g., 20-day) within the requested historical timeframe.
usage: Used to visualize how the consistency of the portfolio's tracking has evolved over time, identifying if the portfolio is becoming more or less correlated with the benchmark.

name: Up/Down Capture Ratio Analysis
description: Calculates the Upside Capture Ratio and Downside Capture Ratio of the portfolio relative to the benchmark to measure how well the portfolio performs during market rallies and sell-offs.
usage: Used to contextualize tracking error by determining if the portfolio's deviation from the benchmark is beneficial (capturing more upside) or detrimental (capturing more downside).

name: Top Positions by Daily Return
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their daily percentage price change, highlighting the securities with the highest positive performance for the current trading session.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions had the largest positive return, which stocks are up the most by percentage today, or requests a ranking of best daily performers.

name: Worst Open Positions by Daily Return
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their lowest daily percentage price change for the current trading session, highlighting the biggest percentage losers.
usage: Used when a user asks for the largest negative return, which stocks are down the most by percentage today, or requests a list of the worst daily performers.

name: Worst Performing Sectors Today
description: Calculates the weighted daily return for each sector represented in the portfolio and ranks them in ascending order to highlight the sectors with the most significant declines.
usage: Used when a user wants to know which industries are dragging down the portfolio the most today or needs to identify sector-specific weakness in the current session.

name: Position Intraday High/Low & Range
description: Retrieves the session high, session low, current price, and calculates the absolute and percentage difference between the high and low for each open position.
usage: Used when a user asks for the high and low of the day, the trading range, or wants to understand the volatility spread of a position during the current session.

name: Top Positions by Intraday Range
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their intraday price range (High - Low) in descending order, highlighting the most volatile securities of the session regardless of direction.
usage: Used to answer which positions had the widest price swings today, which stocks are moving the most intraday, or to identify the highest volatility assets currently held.

name: Lowest Positions by Intraday Range
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their intraday price range in ascending order, highlighting securities that are trading in a tight range or exhibiting low volatility for the session.
usage: Used to find the most stagnant or flat positions for the day, or to identify stocks that are consolidating or exhibiting low intraday volatility.

name: Top Positions by Intraday Volume
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by the total number of shares traded during the current session, highlighting the most actively held securities by share turnover.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions have the highest trading volume today, which stocks are most active right now, or wants to see a ranking of held assets by intraday share activity.

name: Top Positions by Intraday Dollar Volume
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by the total monetary value of shares traded during the current session (Price x Volume), highlighting where the most liquidity is flowing in dollar terms.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions are seeing the most money trade hands today, or wants to rank holdings by notional turnover rather than just share count.

name: Lowest Positions by Intraday Volume
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by the total number of shares traded during the current session in ascending order, highlighting the least active securities.
usage: Used when a user asks for the positions with the lowest trading volume today, which stocks are the least active, or wants to identify holdings with minimal turnover.

name: Lowest Positions by Intraday Dollar Volume
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by the total monetary value of shares traded during the current session (Price x Volume) in ascending order.
usage: Used to determine which holdings have the lowest liquidity in dollar terms today, or to assess where trading large blocks of size might face the most significant price impact relative to the portfolio.

name: Below Average Intraday Volume Filter
description: Filters and lists open positions where the current session's trading volume is lower than a specific threshold relative to its historical average (e.g., < 50% of 30-day average).
usage: Used to identify positions experiencing unusually low activity today compared to their typical behavior, which might indicate a lack of interest or news flow.

name: Position Gap Analysis
description: Calculates the absolute and percentage difference between the current session's opening price and the previous session's closing price for each open position.
usage: Used to determine the specific magnitude and direction of the 'gap' at the open, distinguishing overnight price movement from intraday trading activity.

name: Top Positions by Gap Up Percentage
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by the largest positive percentage difference between today's opening price and yesterday's closing price.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions gapped up the most, opened strong, or had the most significant positive price dislocation at the bell.

name: Top Positions by Gap Down Percentage
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by the largest negative percentage difference between today's opening price and yesterday's closing price.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions gapped down the most, opened weak, or experienced the most significant negative price dislocation at the open.

name: Top Positions by Gap Down Absolute Value
description: Ranks open positions by the largest negative absolute price difference (points or currency value) between today's opening price and yesterday's closing price.
usage: Used when assessing the magnitude of gap downs in terms of absolute price movement rather than percentage, which is useful for high-priced securities where a small percentage drop represents a large point value.

name: Significant Gap Down Filter
description: Filters and lists all open positions where the opening price declined by a user-defined percentage or absolute threshold relative to the previous session's close.
usage: Used when a user wants to screen for all positions experiencing a material negative gap (e.g., 'show me everything that opened down more than 5%') rather than just viewing a ranked list of the top movers.

name: Sector Gap Down Analysis
description: Aggregates the average gap down percentage and absolute value by industry sector based on the opening prices of all held positions relative to the previous close.
usage: Used to determine if negative open sentiment is concentrated in specific sectors or is broad-based across the market, helping to identify if a gap down in a position is idiosyncratic or systemic.

name: Position Level 20-Day Moving Average
description: Calculates the 20-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) for each open position in the portfolio, providing the specific average price level over the last four trading weeks.
usage: Used when a user specifically requests the 20-day moving average value for their holdings, which is a common short-term trend indicator distinct from the 50-day or 200-day averages already tracked.

name: Price vs 20-Day Moving Average Status
description: Determines whether the current market price of an open position is trading above or below its 20-day moving average, and calculates the percentage difference.
usage: Used to identify short-term momentum strength or weakness, specifically answering questions about which stocks are trading above or below their 20-day trend line.

name: Positions Below 20-Day MA Filter
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities currently trading below their 20-day moving average.
usage: Used to quickly isolate short-term bearish positions or identify stocks pulling back from their short-term trend line.

name: Portfolio 20-Day MA Breadth
description: Calculates the percentage and count of open positions trading above versus below their 20-day moving average to indicate the short-term trend health of the portfolio.
usage: Used to get a high-level snapshot of portfolio momentum, answering questions like 'What percent of my portfolio is in a short-term downtrend?'

name: 20-Day Moving Average Slope
description: Calculates the slope or rate of change of the 20-day moving average line to determine if the short-term trend is accelerating up, decelerating, or turning down.
usage: Used to distinguish between a short-term pullback in an uptrend (MA slope is positive) versus the start of a downtrend (MA slope turns negative), even if price is below the MA in both cases.

name: Significant Deviation from 20-Day MA Filter
description: Identifies positions where the current price deviates from the 20-day moving average by a user-defined percentage threshold (e.g., >5% below), highlighting extreme short-term overextensions.
usage: Used to find deeply oversold positions or mean-reversion candidates that have moved significantly away from their short-term average.

name: Price vs 50-Day Moving Average Status
description: Determines whether the current market price of an open position is trading above or below its 50-day moving average and calculates the percentage difference.
usage: Used to identify medium-term trend strength, specifically answering which stocks are trading above or below their 50-day trend line.

name: Positions Above 50-Day Moving Average Filter
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities currently trading above their 50-day simple moving average.
usage: Used to quickly isolate positions in a medium-term uptrend or identify stocks showing positive momentum relative to the 10-week benchmark.

name: Positions Below 50-Day Moving Average Filter
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities currently trading below their 50-day simple moving average.
usage: Used to identify positions in a medium-term correction or downtrend, highlighting potential weakness or mean-reversion opportunities.

name: Position Level 50-Day Moving Average
description: Calculates the 50-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) for each open position in the portfolio, providing the specific average price level over the last ten trading weeks.
usage: Used when a user specifically requests the 50-day moving average value for their holdings, a key benchmark for medium-term technical analysis distinct from the 20-day or 200-day averages.

name: 50-Day Moving Average Slope
description: Calculates the slope or rate of change of the 50-day moving average line to determine if the medium-term trend is accelerating up, decelerating, or turning down.
usage: Used to distinguish between a medium-term pullback in an uptrend (50-day MA slope is positive) versus the start of a downtrend (50-day MA slope turns negative), providing context for positions trading below the 50-day MA.

name: Portfolio 50-Day MA Breadth
description: Calculates the percentage and count of open positions trading above versus below their 50-day moving average to indicate the medium-term trend health of the portfolio.
usage: Used to get a high-level snapshot of portfolio momentum, answering questions like 'What percent of my portfolio is in a medium-term downtrend?' alongside the specific list of positions.

name: Significant Deviation from 50-Day MA Filter
description: Identifies positions where the current price deviates from the 50-day moving average by a user-defined percentage threshold (e.g., >5% below), highlighting extreme medium-term overextensions.
usage: Used to find deeply oversold positions relative to their medium-term trend, filtering the list of positions below the 50-day MA to only those showing significant mean-reversion potential or capitulation.

name: Price vs 200-Day Moving Average Status
description: Determines whether the current market price of an open position is trading above or below its 200-day moving average and calculates the percentage difference.
usage: Used to identify long-term trend strength or weakness, specifically answering questions about which stocks are trading above or below their 200-day trend line.

name: Positions Above 200-Day Moving Average Filter
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities currently trading above their 200-day simple moving average.
usage: Used to quickly isolate positions in a long-term uptrend or identify stocks showing positive momentum relative to the 40-week benchmark.

name: Positions Below 200-Day Moving Average Filter
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities currently trading below their 200-day simple moving average.
usage: Used to identify positions in a long-term correction or downtrend, highlighting potential weakness or breakdown in long-term structure.

name: 200-Day Moving Average Slope
description: Calculates the slope or rate of change of the 200-day moving average line to determine if the long-term trend is accelerating up, decelerating, or turning down.
usage: Used to determine if a position below its 200-day moving average is in a temporary correction within a bull market (positive slope) or a confirmed bear market (negative slope).

name: Duration Below 200-Day Moving Average
description: Calculates the number of consecutive trading days a position has traded below its 200-day moving average.
usage: Used to assess how long a stock has been in a long-term downtrend or correction, helping to distinguish fresh breakdowns from established laggards.

name: Top Positions Furthest Below 200-Day Moving Average
description: Ranks open positions by the percentage deviation below their 200-day moving average, highlighting the securities with the most significant negative long-term divergence.
usage: Used when a user asks which stocks are the most oversold relative to their long-term trend or wants to identify the weakest positions in the portfolio based on 200-day MA distance.

name: Price vs 100-Day Moving Average Status
description: Determines whether the current market price is trading above or below the 100-day moving average and calculates the percentage difference.
usage: Used to analyze intermediate-term trend health, specifically for positions that are below the 200-day MA but might still be holding above the 100-day MA support level.

name: Recent Golden Cross Identification
description: Identifies open positions where the 50-day Moving Average has crossed above the 200-day Moving Average within a specific user-defined lookback window (e.g., the last 10 or 20 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks which positions recently signaled a Golden Cross, entered a new long-term uptrend, or experienced a bullish 50/200 crossover in the short term.

name: Last Moving Average Crossover Event
description: Determines the most recent date and type (Golden or Death Cross) of the crossover between a short-term (e.g., 50-day) and long-term (e.g., 200-day) moving average for each position.
usage: Used to determine exactly when a trend changed for a specific holding, or to verify if the current trend is the result of a recent event or a signal established months ago.

name: Moving Average Spread Analysis
description: Calculates the percentage and absolute difference between the short-term and long-term moving averages to quantify the separation between the trend lines.
usage: Used to assess the strength or 'maturity' of a Golden Cross trend; a wider spread indicates a stronger established trend, while a narrow spread might indicate a weak or fresh signal.

name: Recent Death Cross Identification
description: Identifies open positions where the 50-day Moving Average has crossed below the 200-day Moving Average within a specific user-defined lookback window (e.g., the last 10 or 20 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks which positions recently signaled a Death Cross, entered a new long-term downtrend, or experienced a bearish 50/200 crossover in the short term.

name: Moving Average Crossover Convergence Warning
description: Identifies open positions where the 50-day Moving Average and 200-day Moving Average are within a narrow percentage range of each other, indicating that a Golden or Death Cross event may be imminent.
usage: Used when a user wants to identify positions approaching a major trend change signal, or filter for stocks where the long and short-term MAs are converging.

name: RSI Threshold Filter
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the Relative Strength Index (RSI) meets a specific numerical criteria (e.g., greater than 70 for overbought or less than 30 for oversold).
usage: Used when a user asks for positions with RSI above a certain level, which stocks are overbought, or needs to screen for momentum extremes.

name: Top Positions by Highest RSI
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their current RSI value in descending order, highlighting the securities with the strongest short-term momentum or most overextended prices.
usage: Used when a user wants to see a ranked list of the most overbought stocks, or asks 'Which stock has the highest RSI?' to gauge potential reversal points.

name: Top Positions by Lowest RSI
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their 14-day RSI value in ascending order, highlighting the most oversold securities in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user wants to see a ranked list of the most oversold stocks, asks 'Which position has the lowest RSI?', or needs to identify the most extreme momentum pullbacks to prioritize review.

name: RSI Oversold Duration
description: Calculates the number of consecutive trading days a position's 14-day RSI has remained below the 30 threshold.
usage: Used to determine if an oversold condition (RSI < 30) is a fresh development or a persistent state, helping to distinguish between a potential reversal setup and continued downtrend momentum.

name: RSI Historical Percentile
description: Determines the percentile rank of the current 14-day RSI relative to the stock's own RSI history over the past year (e.g., 252 trading days).
usage: Used to assess how statistically significant the current low RSI is for a specific position, answering if the current oversold reading is rare or common for that particular stock.

name: ATR Percentage of Price Filter
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the Average True Range (ATR) represents a specific percentage or greater of the current stock price, identifying high volatility relative to share price.
usage: Used when a user asks for positions with ATR above a certain percent of price, wants to find stocks with high normalized volatility, or needs to screen for stocks moving X% daily on average.

name: ATR Percentage of Price Breakdown
description: Calculates the Average True Range (ATR) as a percentage of the current market price for every open position in the portfolio, allowing for normalized volatility comparison across assets.
usage: Used when a user wants to see the ATR relative to price for all holdings, compare volatility between high-priced and low-priced stocks, or determine which stocks have the widest percentage swings.

name: Top Positions by ATR Percentage
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their Average True Range expressed as a percentage of current price, highlighting the securities with the highest normalized volatility.
usage: Used when a user wants to know which positions are the most volatile relative to their price, or asks for a ranked list of holdings by average daily movement percentage.

name: ATR Percentage Below Threshold Filter
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the Average True Range (ATR) represents a specific percentage or less of the current stock price, identifying low volatility or consolidation relative to price.
usage: Used when a user asks for positions with ATR below a certain percent of price, wants to find consolidating stocks, or needs to screen for low-volatility assets that are trading in a tight range.

name: Lowest Positions by ATR Percentage
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their Average True Range expressed as a percentage of current price in ascending order, highlighting the securities with the lowest normalized volatility.
usage: Used when a user wants to see a ranked list of the least volatile stocks, asks which positions are the most stagnant, or needs to identify the tightest ranging assets in the portfolio.

name: Position Volume Moving Averages
description: Calculates the Simple Moving Average (SMA) of daily trading volume for multiple lookback periods (e.g., 5-day, 20-day, 50-day) for open positions.
usage: Used to establish volume baselines for individual holdings, enabling comparisons of current trading activity against historical averages.

name: Volume Trend Ratio Analysis
description: Compares short-term average volume to long-term average volume to calculate a ratio that indicates if volume is expanding (ratio > 1) or contracting (ratio < 1).
usage: Used to identify positions experiencing rising or falling interest, verify if price breakouts are supported by volume, or answer questions about increasing volume trends over specific periods like 4 weeks.

name: Volume Trend Linear Slope
description: Computes the linear regression slope of the daily trading volume over a specified historical period to quantify the rate of increase or decrease in volume.
usage: Used to determine the consistency of volume trends over time (e.g., last 4 weeks), distinguishing between steady growth and sporadic spikes to identify accumulation or distribution.

name: Volume Trend Direction Filter
description: Filters open positions to identify those exhibiting a specific volume trend direction (increasing or decreasing) over a defined lookback period (e.g., 4 weeks) based on the linear regression slope of daily volume.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions have falling volume, increasing volume, or want to screen for liquidity contraction and expansion trends over a specific timeframe.

name: Top Positions by Historical Single-Day Gain
description: Identifies and ranks open positions based on the highest single-day percentage return achieved within a specified historical lookback window (e.g., 30 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks which positions had the largest single-day gain in the last month, or wants to find stocks in their portfolio that recently experienced a significant positive momentum spike.

name: Position Historical Daily Return Extremes
description: Calculates the specific date, value, and magnitude of the best single-day gain and the worst single-day loss for a specific position over a historical period.
usage: Used to identify the specific days of extreme volatility or performance for a holding, answering questions like 'What was the best day for TSLA in the last quarter?' or 'When did this stock have its biggest drop?'

name: Top Positions by Historical Single-Day Point Gain
description: Ranks open positions by the largest absolute price point increase achieved in a single day within a historical window, distinct from percentage gain.
usage: Used when a user is interested in the absolute price movement (e.g., 'Which stock gained the most points in one day?') rather than the percentage return, which is relevant for high-share-price positions.

name: Top Positions by Historical Single-Day Loss
description: Identifies and ranks open positions based on the lowest single-day percentage return (largest drop) achieved within a specified historical lookback window (e.g., 30 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks which positions had the largest single-day drop in the last month, or wants to find stocks in their portfolio that recently experienced a significant negative momentum spike or crash.

name: Top Positions by Historical Single-Day Point Loss
description: Ranks open positions by the largest absolute price point decrease achieved in a single day within a historical window (e.g., 30 trading days), distinct from percentage loss.
usage: Used when a user asks which stock lost the most points in one day recently, focusing on absolute price movement rather than percentage return, which is relevant for high-share-price positions.

name: Position Custom Period Return Calculation
description: Calculates the total percentage return for each open position over a user-defined historical timeframe (e.g., 1 month, 3 months, YTD), distinct from cost-basis P&L.
usage: Used when asking for performance over a specific lookback window (e.g., 'last 3 months'), excluding the lifetime performance from purchase.

name: Positions Above Custom Return Threshold
description: Filters open positions to return only those that have achieved a return percentage greater than or equal to a specified threshold over a defined historical period.
usage: Used to answer questions like 'Which stocks are up more than 20% in the last 3 months?' or 'Show me my winners from the last year.'

name: Top Positions by Custom Period Return
description: Ranks open positions by their total percentage return over a specific historical timeframe in descending order.
usage: Used to identify the best performers within a specific window, distinct from 'Top Positions by Unrealized Gain' which measures lifetime performance.

name: Positions Below Custom Return Threshold
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the total return over a specified historical timeframe (e.g., 3 months) is less than a specific negative threshold (e.g., -20%).
usage: Used when a user asks for positions down more than a certain amount, which stocks are losing money over a specific period, or wants to isolate underperformers within a custom date range.

name: Position Level Bid-Ask Spread
description: Calculates the current absolute and percentage difference between the bid and ask price for every open position.
usage: Used to assess current liquidity, estimated trading costs, and market tightness for specific holdings at this exact moment.

name: Historical Average Bid-Ask Spread
description: Calculates the mean absolute and percentage bid-ask spread for each open position over a specified historical period (e.g., 5 trading days).
usage: Used to determine the typical trading cost and liquidity profile of holdings over recent history, smoothing out intraday spikes or widenings.

name: Tightest Average Spread Positions
