description: Ranks open positions by their lowest average bid-ask spread (absolute or percentage) over a specified historical period, highlighting the most liquid assets.
usage: Used when a user wants to identify the positions with the lowest trading costs, highest liquidity, or 'tightest' markets over a specific timeframe.

name: Widest Average Spread Positions
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by their highest average bid-ask spread (absolute or percentage) over a specified historical period.
usage: Used when a user asks for positions with the widest spreads, highest trading costs, or least liquidity over a specific timeframe (e.g., last 5 days).

name: Level I Market Quote
description: Provides the real-time National Best Bid and Offer (NBBO) and the last trade price for a specified ticker, including share sizes.
usage: Used when a user asks for the current price, market quote, bid/ask spread, or the exact price of the last trade for a specific symbol.

name: Extended Hours Quote
description: Provides the current bid, ask, and last trade price specifically for pre-market and after-hours trading sessions, distinct from regular session prices.
usage: Used when a user asks for pre-market or after-hours quotes, or wants to see price activity outside of standard market hours (9:30 AM - 4:00 PM ET).

name: Intraday Session Statistics
description: Provides the real-time trading range and cumulative volume for the current session for a specified ticker symbol.
usage: Used when a user asks for today's high, low, volume, or open price for a specific security, regardless of whether it is currently held in the portfolio.

name: Ticker Historical Average Bid-Ask Spread
description: Calculates the average absolute and percentage bid-ask spread for a specific market symbol over a defined historical period (e.g., 5 trading days), regardless of whether the security is held in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user asks for the historical liquidity or trading costs of a specific stock they are researching or monitoring (e.g., 'What was the average spread for NVDA last week?'), which is distinct from analyzing the spreads of currently held positions.

name: Symbol 52-Week High & Low
description: Retrieves the 52-week high and low prices for any specified ticker symbol, regardless of whether it is currently held in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user asks for the 52-week high and low of a specific stock they are researching or watching, rather than one they currently own.

name: Symbol Realized Volatility
description: Calculates the annualized standard deviation of daily returns for a specific market symbol over a user-defined lookback period (e.g., 30 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks for the historical volatility of a specific stock they are researching, checking risk levels for a potential trade, or needs volatility data for a symbol not currently held in the portfolio.

name: Symbol Daily Return Series
description: Provides the daily percentage price change time series for a specified ticker over a requested historical period.
usage: Used when a user needs to analyze the daily performance of a specific stock to calculate custom metrics, visualize volatility, or understand the distribution of returns for a symbol not held in the portfolio.

name: Symbol Historical Price Data
description: Retrieves the daily open, high, low, close (OHLC) prices and volume for a specific ticker over a specified historical range.
usage: Used when a user asks for price history, needs chart data, or requires raw OHLC data for a specific symbol to perform technical analysis.

name: Symbol 14-Day RSI
description: Calculates the current 14-day Relative Strength Index (RSI) for a specified ticker symbol to identify momentum strength and potential overbought or oversold conditions.
usage: Used when a user asks for the RSI of a specific stock they are researching or monitoring (e.g., 'What is the RSI for AAPL?'), distinct from the RSI of positions currently held in the portfolio.

name: Symbol 14-Day ATR
description: Calculates the 14-day Average True Range (ATR) for a specified ticker symbol to measure volatility and potential daily price movement.
usage: Used when a user asks for the ATR of a specific stock they are researching or monitoring (e.g., 'What is the ATR for AAPL?'), distinct from the ATR of positions currently held in the portfolio.

name: Symbol 50-Day & 200-Day Moving Averages
description: Returns the current price levels of the 50-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) and the 200-day SMA for a specified ticker symbol.
usage: Used when a user asks for the specific numerical values of the 50-day and 200-day moving averages for a stock they are researching or watching, which is distinct from the moving averages of currently held positions.

name: Symbol 50/200 Moving Average Comparison
description: Determines the relationship between the 50-day and 200-day moving averages for a specified symbol, indicating if the short-term trend is above the long-term trend.
usage: Used to directly answer if a chosen symbol has its 50-day MA above its 200-day MA, indicating a long-term bullish trend (Golden Cross state), or below, indicating a bearish trend.

name: Symbol Daily Price Change
description: Retrieves the current trading price, previous session close, and calculates the daily percentage and absolute price change for a specified ticker.
usage: Used when a user asks for the daily performance of a specific stock they are watching (e.g., 'How much is Apple up today?'), or needs the daily return metric for a symbol not currently held in the portfolio.

name: Symbol Volume Moving Averages
description: Calculates the Simple Moving Average (SMA) of daily trading volume for multiple lookback periods (e.g., 5-day, 20-day, 50-day) for a specified ticker symbol.
usage: Used when a user asks for the average volume of a specific stock they are researching (e.g., 'What is the 20-day average volume for AAPL?'), or wants to compare current volume to historical liquidity baselines for a symbol not currently held in the portfolio.

name: Symbol 52-Week High Distance
description: Calculates the percentage and absolute difference between the current market price and the 52-week high for a specified ticker.
usage: Used when a user asks how far a specific stock is from its yearly high, what the drawdown from the peak is, or wants to quantify the price decline from the 52-week high.

name: Symbol 52-Week Low Distance
description: Calculates the percentage and absolute difference between the current market price and the 52-week low for a specified ticker symbol.
usage: Used when a user asks how far a specific stock is from its yearly low, what the percentage recovery from the bottom is, or wants to quantify the price increase relative to the 52-week low.

name: Symbol Highest Volume Day Identification
description: Identifies the specific date and trading volume count of the single day with the highest volume within a user-defined historical lookback period for a specified ticker.
usage: Used when a user asks 'Which day had the highest volume in the last X days?', 'When was the peak volume?', or wants to identify outlier liquidity events for a specific symbol.

name: Symbol Lowest Volume Day Identification
description: Identifies the specific date and trading volume count of the single day with the lowest volume within a user-defined historical lookback period for a specified ticker.
usage: Used when a user asks 'Which day had the lowest volume?', 'When was liquidity the thinnest?', or wants to identify periods of low interest or consolidation for a specific symbol.

name: Symbol Largest Intraday Range Day Identification
description: Identifies the specific date and calculates the magnitude (absolute and percentage) of the widest trading range (High - Low) for a chosen symbol within a defined historical lookback period (e.g., last 30 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks which specific day had the biggest price swing, the highest volatility day by range, or the day with the largest gap between the high and low within the last month.

name: Symbol Daily Intraday Range History
description: Calculates the daily intraday range (High - Low) for every trading day within a specified historical period for a chosen symbol, presented as a time series.
usage: Used to visualize the trend of daily volatility, rank days by range manually, or analyze the frequency of high-range days over the requested period.

name: Symbol Average Intraday Range Statistics
description: Calculates the mean, median, minimum, and maximum daily intraday range (High - Low) for a chosen symbol over a specified historical period.
usage: Used to contextualize a specific large range day against the average behavior (e.g., 'The range today was 5%, compared to a 1% average'), or to understand the typical daily price width of a security.

name: Symbol Beta vs Benchmark
description: Calculates the beta coefficient for a specific ticker symbol relative to a user-defined benchmark (e.g., SPY, QQQ) over a specified lookback period (e.g., 60 trading days).
usage: Used when researching the systematic risk or market sensitivity of a stock that is not necessarily in the current portfolio, specifically answering questions like 'What is the beta of TSLA?' or 'How volatile is this stock compared to the S&P 500?'

name: Symbol Average Daily Dollar Volume
description: Calculates the average monetary value (Price x Volume) of shares traded daily for a specific ticker over a user-defined lookback period (e.g., 30 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks for the liquidity of a specific stock in dollar terms, how much money trades hands on average, or specifically requests the average daily dollar volume over the last 30 days.

name: Symbol Daily Dollar Volume Series
description: Provides a chronological time series of the calculated daily dollar volume (Closing Price x Volume) for a specific ticker over a specified historical range.
usage: Used to retrieve the underlying data points to calculate custom averages, visualize dollar volume trends over time, or identify specific days with unusually high or low capital flows.

name: Symbol Historical Up/Down Day Count
description: Aggregates daily closing prices for a specific symbol over a user-defined historical period to calculate the total number of sessions that closed positive (up days), negative (down days), or unchanged.
usage: Used when a user asks 'How many up days versus down days occurred in the last 30 trading days?' or wants to determine the win rate of price direction over a specific timeframe.

name: Symbol Consecutive Price Streak
description: Identifies the current streak of consecutive trading days where the price closed in the same direction, as well as the maximum consecutive up and down streaks within the specified period.
usage: Used to determine if the current market trend is persistent (e.g., 'Has it been up 5 days in a row?') and to identify historical consistency in directional movement.

name: Symbol Average Daily Return
description: Calculates the arithmetic mean of the daily percentage returns for a specific ticker over a defined historical lookback period (e.g., 30 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks for the average daily return of a stock, the mean daily performance over the last month, or wants to quantify the typical daily price change magnitude.

name: Symbol Fixed Period Cumulative Return
description: Calculates the total percentage gain or loss for a specific ticker over a defined historical window of trading days (e.g., 30 days), compounding the daily price changes.
usage: Used when a user asks for the total return over a specific recent period (e.g., 'last 30 trading days'), 'how much is X up this month', or needs the aggregated performance metric for a symbol not currently held in the portfolio.

name: Symbol vs Benchmark Relative Return
description: Calculates the difference in cumulative return between a chosen symbol and a benchmark index (e.g., SPY) over the same specified historical period.
usage: Used to determine if a specific stock outperformed or underperformed the market over a given timeframe (e.g., 30 trading days), providing context to the raw return figure.

name: Symbol Rolling Volatility Series
description: Calculates the realized volatility (annualized standard deviation of returns) for a specific ticker using a defined rolling window (e.g., 20 days) across a historical timeframe, returning a time series of values.
usage: Used to visualize the trend of volatility for a specific stock over time, identifying periods of market expansion or contraction distinct from a single point-in-time value.

name: Symbol Volatility Historical Percentile
description: Determines the percentile rank of the current realized volatility for a specific ticker relative to its own volatility history over a defined long-term period (e.g., 1 year).
usage: Used to contextually assess if the current 20-day volatility is high or low compared to the stock's typical behavior, aiding in decisions about option pricing or risk assessment.

name: Symbol Implied Volatility
description: Retrieves the current market-implied volatility derived from the options pricing for a specific ticker, typically representing the expected annualized movement over a specific timeframe (e.g., 30 days).
usage: Used to compare market expectations (future volatility) against historical realized volatility (actual movement) to gauge potential mispricing or upcoming events.

name: Symbol Maximum Drawdown
description: Calculates the largest peak-to-trough decline for a specific security over a defined historical period (e.g., 6 months), expressed as a percentage.
usage: Used when a user asks for the max drawdown, worst-case loss scenario, or largest price drop for a specific stock or asset over a specific timeframe.

name: Symbol Drawdown Time Series
description: Calculates the daily percentage decline from the rolling peak price for a specific security over a historical period, creating an 'underwater' curve to visualize drawdowns over time.
usage: Used when a user wants to see the history of drawdowns, visualize the path to the maximum drawdown, or analyze how frequently the asset trades below its peak.

name: Detailed Consecutive Streaks Log
description: Lists all distinct streaks of consecutive up or down days within the specified historical period, providing the start date, end date, and length for each streak.
usage: Used when a user needs to see the specific sequence of momentum over a period (e.g., 'Show me all the winning streaks last month' or 'Did we have a 5-day rally recently?'), rather than just the single maximum streak provided by standard summaries.

name: Consecutive Streak Length Frequency
description: Aggregates the number of times consecutive up or down streaks of specific lengths occurred within a historical period (e.g., 3 streaks of 2 days, 1 streak of 5 days).
usage: Used to analyze the character of price action, determining if the market is choppy (many 1-day streaks) or trending (fewer, longer streaks) during the period.

name: Average Consecutive Streak Length
description: Calculates the mean length of all consecutive up days and all consecutive down days within a specified historical window.
usage: Used to quantify the typical momentum run, distinguishing between a market that reverses daily (avg ~1.0) and one that trends (avg > 2.0).

name: Symbol Total Days in Consecutive Down Streaks
description: Calculates the aggregate sum of trading days that were part of a consecutive down streak, excluding isolated single down days.
usage: Used to determine the total volume of selling pressure that occurred in clusters rather than isolated drops, answering questions like 'How many days total were part of consecutive losses?'

name: Symbol Down Streak Count
description: Counts the total number of distinct instances where the security closed lower for two or more consecutive trading days.
usage: Used to measure the frequency of negative momentum shifts, answering questions like 'How many separate losing streaks occurred in the last 30 days?'

name: Symbol Down Streak Threshold Occurrence
description: Identifies if a consecutive down streak met or exceeded a specific user-defined length threshold (e.g., 5 days) and counts how many times it happened.
usage: Used to screen for specific adverse market behaviors, answering questions like 'Did it drop 5 days in a row?' or 'How many times was there a streak of at least 3 down days?'

name: Symbol 10-Day Average Intraday Range
description: Calculates the arithmetic mean of the daily intraday price range (High minus Low) specifically for the last 10 trading days.
usage: Used to gauge short-term volatility and typical daily trading width over the most recent two-week period, answering the specific request for a 10-day average.

name: Symbol Rolling Average Intraday Range Series
description: Provides a time series of the average intraday range calculated over a rolling window (e.g., 10 days) across a longer historical timeframe.
usage: Used to visualize the trend of intraday volatility over time, identifying if the market is becoming more or less volatile on a short-term basis.

name: Symbol 10-Day Intraday Range Volatility
description: Calculates the standard deviation of the daily intraday ranges over the last 10 trading days to measure the consistency of daily price swings.
usage: Used to determine if the recent trading range has been stable or erratic, providing context on the reliability of the average range metric.

name: Symbol 10-Day Median Intraday Range
description: Calculates the statistical median of the daily intraday range (High - Low) specifically for the last 10 trading days, providing a measure of typical daily price width that is robust against outlier spikes.
usage: Used when a user asks for the median intraday range over a specific short-term window (e.g., last 10 days) to understand central tendency without the skew of extreme volatility days, distinct from the average (mean).

name: Symbol 20-Day Moving Average
description: Calculates the 20-day simple moving average (SMA) for a specific ticker symbol.
usage: Used when a user specifically requests the 20-day moving average for a stock they are researching, which is distinct from the 50-day or 200-day averages already available for symbols.

name: Symbol 20-Day Standard Deviation
description: Calculates the standard deviation of daily returns over the last 20 trading days for a specific ticker symbol.
usage: Used when a user asks for the 20-day standard deviation of a symbol, typically to assess recent volatility or to calculate indicators like Bollinger Bands (which use raw SD rather than annualized realized volatility).

name: Symbol Gap-Up Event Count
description: Counts the number of trading sessions within a specified lookback period (e.g., 20 days) where the opening price was higher than the previous session's closing price.
usage: Used to determine the frequency of positive overnight price movement for a specific stock, answering questions like 'How many gap-ups occurred in the last 20 days?'

name: Symbol Gap-Down Event Count
description: Counts the number of trading sessions within a specified lookback period where the opening price was lower than the previous session's closing price.
usage: Used to determine the frequency of negative overnight price movement for a specific stock, often used in conjunction with gap-up analysis to gauge overnight sentiment volatility.

name: Symbol Gap Event Log
description: Provides a detailed list of specific dates within a historical period where a gap-up or gap-down occurred, including the opening price, previous close, and the calculated percentage magnitude for each event.
usage: Used when a user needs to see the exact dates of gaps and their respective sizes to analyze specific events like earnings, news releases, or market shocks.

name: Symbol Average Gap Down Magnitude
description: Calculates the mean, median, minimum, and maximum percentage gap (open price vs previous close) for all gap-down events occurring within the specified period.
usage: Used when a user needs to understand the severity of the gap downs, not just the frequency. It answers 'How big were these gap downs on average?' or 'What was the worst drop at the open?'.

name: Symbol Rolling Gap Down Frequency Series
description: Calculates the number of gap-down events over a rolling window (e.g., 20 trading days) across a historical timeframe, returning a time series of counts.
usage: Used to determine if the current frequency of gap downs is historically high or low, providing context to the 'how many' question by showing the trend over time.

name: Symbol Gap Down Intraday Recovery Rate
description: Analyzes the intraday performance of days that opened with a gap down to determine how often the price closed above the opening price or filled the gap.
usage: Used to assess buying interest following negative gaps. It answers 'Do these gap downs usually recover by the end of the day?' or 'How often is a gap down faded?'

name: Account Watchlist Directory
description: Provides a high-level list of all watchlists associated with the user's account, including metadata such as watchlist names, unique IDs, and the total count of symbols contained within each.
usage: Used when a user asks for a list of their watchlists, wants to see how many watchlists they have, or needs to retrieve the specific names/IDs of their lists before viewing the contents.

name: Watchlist Symbol Composition
description: Retrieves the full list of ticker symbols contained within a specific user-defined watchlist.
usage: Used when a user asks what stocks are in a specific watchlist (e.g., 'Tech Stocks'), wants to see the contents of a particular list, or needs to verify if a symbol is already on a list.

name: Complete Watchlist Inventory
description: Aggregates all watchlists and their respective symbol contents into a single payload, providing a comprehensive mapping of every list to its tickers.
usage: Used when a user wants to see 'everything at a glance' or asks specifically 'What watchlists do I have and what is in them?', requiring a full dump of the user's saved list structures.

name: Largest Watchlist by Symbol Count
description: Identifies the specific watchlist with the highest number of ticker symbols, returning the watchlist name and the total symbol count.
usage: Used when a user asks which watchlist is the largest, contains the most stocks, or has the highest number of items.

name: Smallest Watchlist by Symbol Count
description: Identifies the specific watchlist with the lowest number of ticker symbols, providing the watchlist name, unique ID, and the total count of items.
usage: Used when a user asks for the smallest watchlist, the list with the fewest stocks, or the least populated watchlist.

name: Watchlist Size Ranked List
description: Ranks all watchlists in the account by the total number of symbols they contain, listing them in ascending or descending order of size.
usage: Used to view the distribution of watchlist sizes at a glance, identify both the smallest and largest lists, or compare the scale of different saved lists.

name: Total Unique Watchlist Symbol Count
description: Calculates the aggregate number of distinct ticker symbols found across all user-defined watchlists, automatically deduplicating any symbols that appear in multiple lists to provide a single total count.
usage: Used when a user asks for the total number of unique stocks they are tracking across all lists, or wants to quantify the breadth of their market coverage without double-counting overlaps.

name: Top Performing Symbols in Watchlist
description: Identifies and ranks the symbols within a specific watchlist that generated the highest cumulative return over a defined timeframe (e.g., 30 trading days).
usage: Used to answer questions like 'Which stock in my watchlist performed best this month?' or 'Who are the top winners in my Tech list?'

name: Watchlist Performance Breakdown
description: Calculates and lists the cumulative return for every symbol within a specific watchlist over a defined timeframe, providing a comprehensive view of individual performance for all list items.
usage: Used to review the performance of all tracked symbols in a list, not just the top or bottom extremes, to see which tickers are green versus red over the period.

name: Top Performing Symbols Across All Watchlists
description: Aggregates the cumulative returns for all symbols contained across every user-defined watchlist and ranks them to identify the absolute top performers within the entire watchlist universe.
usage: Used when a user wants to find the single best performing stock across all of their lists, or asks 'Which of my tracked stocks had the highest return?' without specifying a particular watchlist.

name: Worst Performing Symbols in Watchlist
description: Identifies and ranks the symbols within a specific watchlist that generated the lowest cumulative return (highest loss) over a defined timeframe (e.g., 30 trading days).
usage: Used to answer questions like 'Which stock in my watchlist performed worst this month?' or 'Who are the bottom performers in my Tech list?'

name: Worst Performing Symbols Across All Watchlists
description: Aggregates the cumulative returns for all symbols contained across every user-defined watchlist and ranks them to identify the absolute worst performers within the entire watchlist universe.
usage: Used when a user wants to find the single worst performing stock across all of their lists, or asks 'Which of my tracked stocks had the lowest return?' without specifying a particular watchlist.

name: Top Volume Symbols in Watchlist
description: Identifies and ranks the symbols within a specific watchlist by the total number of shares traded during the current trading session.
usage: Used when a user asks which stocks in their watchlist have the highest volume today, which ticker is seeing the most activity, or specifically requests a ranking of watchlist items by intraday volume.

name: Watchlist Intraday Volume Breakdown
description: Provides a list of all symbols in a specific watchlist along with their current session trading volume, allowing for a comprehensive view of activity levels across the list.
usage: Used when a user wants to see the volume for every symbol in a specific list rather than just the top performers, or needs to screen for volume thresholds across a group of tracked stocks.

name: Top Volume Symbols Across All Watchlists
description: Aggregates the current session volume for all symbols contained across every user-defined watchlist and ranks them to identify the absolute most active stocks within the user's entire tracking universe.
usage: Used when a user wants to find the single highest volume stock across all their lists, or asks 'Which of my tracked stocks is trading the most right now?' without specifying a particular watchlist.

name: Watchlist Relative Volume Analysis
description: Calculates the ratio of the current session's trading volume to the historical average volume (e.g., 30-day average) for each symbol in a watchlist to identify unusual activity.
usage: Used when a user wants to find stocks in their watchlist that are trading significantly higher than normal (e.g., 'Which stocks are popping with volume today?'), rather than just those with the highest absolute volume.

name: Top Intraday Range Symbols in Watchlist
description: Identifies and ranks the symbols within a specific watchlist by the largest absolute or percentage difference between the current session's high and low prices.
usage: Used when a user asks which stock in a specific list is moving the most today, or requests a ranking of watchlist items by intraday price swing.

name: Top Intraday Range Symbols Across All Watchlists
description: Aggregates the intraday range data for all symbols contained across every user-defined watchlist and ranks them to identify the absolute highest volatility ticker being tracked.
usage: Used when a user wants to find the single most volatile stock across all of their lists without specifying a particular watchlist.

name: Watchlist Intraday Range Breakdown
description: Provides a list of all symbols in a specific watchlist along with their current session's high, low, and calculated intraday range.
usage: Used when a user wants to see the intraday volatility for every symbol in a list to identify multiple movers, rather than just the top ranking result.

name: Top Gap Up Symbols in Watchlist
description: Identifies and ranks the symbols within a specific watchlist that opened higher than the previous session's close, sorted by the largest gap up percentage or absolute value.
usage: Used to answer questions like 'Which stock in my watchlist gapped up the most today?' or 'What are the biggest gap ups in my Tech list?'.

name: Top Gap Up Symbols Across All Watchlists
description: Aggregates the gap analysis for all symbols contained across every user-defined watchlist and ranks them to identify the absolute largest gap up movers being tracked.
usage: Used when a user wants to find the single largest gap up across all their lists without specifying a particular watchlist, or asks 'Which of my tracked stocks gapped up the most today?'.

name: Watchlist Gap Analysis Breakdown
description: Provides a comprehensive list of all symbols in a specific watchlist along with their calculated gap (percentage and absolute) for the current session, distinguishing between gap ups and gap downs.
usage: Used to view the full sentiment of a watchlist at the open, seeing which stocks gapped up, which gapped down, and by how much, rather than just the top rankers.

name: Top Gap Down Symbols in Watchlist
description: Identifies and ranks the symbols within a specific watchlist that opened lower than the previous session's close, sorted by the largest gap down percentage or absolute value.
usage: Used to answer questions like 'Which stock in my watchlist gapped down the most today?' or 'What are the biggest gap downs in my Tech list?'.

name: Top Gap Down Symbols Across All Watchlists
description: Aggregates the gap analysis for all symbols contained across every user-defined watchlist and ranks them to identify the absolute largest gap down movers being tracked.
usage: Used when a user wants to find the single largest gap down across all of their lists without specifying a particular watchlist, or asks 'Which of my tracked stocks gapped down the most today?'.

name: Watchlist-less Open Orders List
description: Identifies and lists all currently active open orders where the underlying ticker symbol is not present in any of the user's saved watchlists.
usage: Used when a user wants to identify active trading activity for stocks they are not actively tracking on a watchlist, helping to catch 'rogue' trades or gaps in monitoring coverage.

name: Unwatched Symbols in Open Orders
description: Extracts and deduplicates the ticker symbols from all open orders, returning a unique list of only those symbols that do not appear in any existing watchlist.
usage: Used to quickly identify which specific stocks are being traded but not tracked, allowing the user to easily add them to a watchlist or decide if the exposure is intentional.

name: Unwatched Open Order Count
description: Calculates the total count of active open orders associated with symbols that are not included in any user watchlist.
usage: Used to provide a quick summary metric indicating how much active trading is occurring outside of the user's tracked watchlists.

name: Watchlist Symbols Held in Portfolio
description: Identifies and lists the specific symbols that are present in both the user's watchlists and their current open positions.
usage: Used to find the overlap between what is being tracked and what is actually owned, answering questions like 'Which of my watchlist stocks do I currently own?'

name: Unwatched Open Positions List
description: Identifies and lists open positions where the ticker symbol does not appear in any of the user's saved watchlists.
usage: Used to find 'orphan' holdings that are owned but not being actively tracked on a watchlist, complementing the intersection data.

name: Watchlist Symbol Ownership Status
description: Provides a status update for every symbol across all watchlists indicating whether the user currently holds an open position in that stock.
usage: Used to review a watchlist and see which tracked ideas have been executed into actual positions versus which are just being monitored.

name: Unheld Watchlist Symbols List
description: Filters and lists ticker symbols from the user's watchlists that are not present in the current open positions, effectively identifying a 'shopping list' or tracked ideas not yet owned.
usage: Used when a user wants to see which stocks they are watching but have not yet bought, or to review tracked opportunities that are not yet part of the portfolio.

name: Unheld Watchlist Symbol Count
description: Calculates the total number of unique symbols within watchlists that do not have a corresponding open position in the portfolio.
usage: Used to provide a quick summary metric of how many tracked ideas are currently unexecuted, either globally across all watchlists or for a specific list.

name: Position Upcoming Earnings Schedule
description: Retrieves the next scheduled earnings announcement date and time (Pre-Market, During Market, or After-Hours) for each open position in the portfolio.
usage: Used to determine when a specific holding is next reporting financial results, calculating the exact days remaining until the event.

name: Upcoming Earnings Filter
description: Filters the open positions list to identify only those stocks with earnings announcements scheduled within a user-defined future timeframe (e.g., the next 14 days).
usage: Used to isolate portfolio risk tied to imminent corporate events, directly answering questions like 'What do I have coming up in the next two weeks?'

name: Earnings Consensus Estimates
description: Provides the analyst consensus estimates for Earnings Per Share (EPS) and Revenue for the upcoming fiscal quarter of a specific security.
usage: Used when a user wants to know what the 'street' expects the company to report, which is essential context for evaluating post-earnings volatility.

name: Historical Earnings Price Volatility
description: Calculates the average absolute percentage price change a stock experiences on the day of its last few earnings reports (e.g., last 4 quarters).
usage: Used to estimate potential risk or opportunity around an upcoming earnings date by analyzing how volatile the stock has historically been during these events.

name: Positions with Recent Earnings
description: Filters the portfolio's open positions to identify only those securities that released an earnings report within a specified historical lookback window (e.g., the last 30 days).
usage: Used when a user asks 'Which positions had earnings in the last 30 days?', 'Did any of my holdings report recently?', or wants to identify specific stocks that just completed an earnings cycle.

name: Position Historical Earnings Log
description: Retrieves a chronological list of historical earnings report dates for each open position, typically covering the last 4 to 8 quarters.
usage: Used when a user needs to know the specific dates of past earnings reports for their holdings, regardless of how recent they were, to analyze seasonality or past event timing.

name: Symbol Historical Earnings Dates
description: Retrieves the dates of recent past earnings reports for a specific ticker symbol, regardless of whether it is held in the portfolio.
usage: Used when researching the earnings history of a stock the user is watching or considering buying, to see when it typically reports or when the last report occurred.

name: Position Upcoming Ex-Dividend Schedule
description: Retrieves the next scheduled ex-dividend date, record date, and payment date for each open position in the portfolio.
usage: Used to determine the specific dates when a holding will trade without the dividend, when ownership is recorded, and when the payout will be received.

name: Upcoming Ex-Dividend Filter
description: Filters the open positions list to identify only those securities with an ex-dividend date scheduled within a user-defined future timeframe (e.g., next 14 days).
usage: Used to directly answer questions like 'What goes ex-dividend next week?' or to identify positions that must be held by a certain date to qualify for the next payout.

name: Estimated Upcoming Dividend Income
description: Calculates the projected cash payout for the next dividend event of each open position by multiplying the declared dividend per share by the number of shares owned.
usage: Used to forecast the total cash inflow from upcoming dividend payments, helping the user understand the income value of the positions identified in the ex-dividend schedule.

name: Recent Dividend Payments by Current Position
description: Filters dividend transaction history to specifically list payments received for securities currently held in the portfolio within a user-defined lookback period.
usage: Used when a user asks which of their current positions paid a dividend in a recent timeframe (e.g., 'last 30 days'), distinguishing income from current holdings from historical income of closed positions.

name: Position Level Indicated Dividend Yield
description: Calculates the dividend yield for each individual open position based on the latest annualized dividend rate and current market price.
usage: Used when a user asks for the dividend yield of their specific holdings, wants to see the income return percentage for each stock, or needs to verify the yield of a particular position.

name: Top Positions by Dividend Yield
description: Ranks open positions by their current indicated dividend yield percentage in descending order, highlighting the highest income-generating assets.
usage: Used when a user wants to identify the highest yielding stocks in their portfolio, find their biggest income payers, or sort holdings by yield percentage.

name: Sector Dividend Yield Breakdown
description: Calculates the weighted average dividend yield for the portfolio's holdings within each industry sector.
usage: Used to analyze which sectors are providing the highest yield returns within the portfolio, distinct from the total dollar amount of dividend income.

name: Lowest Positions by Dividend Yield
description: Ranks open positions by their current indicated dividend yield percentage in ascending order, highlighting the lowest yielding or non-yielding assets.
usage: Used when a user wants to identify stocks with little to no dividend income, or isolate growth stocks that do not pay dividends within their portfolio.

name: Open Positions Last Dividend
description: Retrieves the most recent dividend payment details (amount and pay date) for every security currently held in the portfolio.
usage: Used when a user asks for the last dividend received for their stocks, the most recent payout history for current holdings, or specifically needs the date and amount of the latest dividend payment per position.

name: Total Holdings Market Value
description: Calculates the aggregate market value of all open positions in the portfolio, explicitly excluding cash and cash equivalents to show the total capital currently deployed in the market.
usage: Used when a user asks for the value of their investments excluding cash, their invested capital, or the total market value of their positions without including idle cash.

name: Account Equity Historical Series
description: Provides a time series of the total account value (equity) combining cash and securities over a specified historical period (e.g., daily, weekly, or monthly closing values).
usage: Used when a user wants to view the historical growth curve of their total portfolio value, track net worth trends over time, or visualize how the 'current value' has changed in the past.

name: Account Equity Daily Change
description: Calculates the absolute dollar amount and percentage change of the total account equity compared to the previous trading day's closing value.
usage: Used to determine how much the total portfolio value (including cash) increased or decreased today, providing a direct answer to 'How much am I up or down today in total?'

name: Account Equity Change Attribution
description: Decomposes the change in total account equity over a specific period into its constituent drivers: Realized P&L, Unrealized P&L change, and Net Cash Flow (deposits/withdrawals).
usage: Used to understand *why* the portfolio value changed (e.g., did it grow because of market performance or because I deposited money?), bridging the gap between the starting value and ending value.

name: Projected Post-Execution Net Exposure
description: Simulates the portfolio's long and short market value exposure by combining current holdings with the notional value of all open orders, assuming full execution at their specified prices.
usage: Used to determine what the portfolio's net long vs. short exposure will be after all pending trades fill, answering the question of how order execution changes the account's directional market risk.

name: Pending Order Net Impact Summary
description: Calculates the isolated net change in exposure specifically attributable to open orders, independent of the current portfolio state.
usage: Used to understand the specific directional pressure of working orders—whether they are adding to long exposure, covering shorts, or opening new short positions—without recalculating the entire portfolio balance.

name: Projected Post-Fill Beta-Weighted Exposure
description: Estimates the portfolio's aggregate beta-weighted exposure relative to a benchmark (e.g., SPY) after all open orders are executed and filled.
usage: Used to assess how pending trades will alter the portfolio's systematic risk profile or correlation to the market, specifically calculating the projected delta of market exposure.

name: Maintenance Margin Requirement Calculation
description: Calculates the specific total dollar amount of equity required to be maintained in the account to hold the current open positions, based on regulatory and broker-specific margin rules.
usage: Used when a user asks for their current maintenance margin requirement, the minimum equity needed to avoid a margin call, or the specific dollar threshold for their existing holdings.

name: Initial Margin Requirement Calculation
description: Calculates the total initial margin requirement for all currently open positions in the portfolio, representing the capital required to establish these positions under current margin rules.
usage: Used when a user asks for their initial margin requirement, the margin needed to open their current positions, or wants to compare initial requirements against maintenance requirements to assess margin cushion.

name: Buying Power Impact of Open Orders
description: Calculates the total amount of buying power currently locked up or reserved by all open working orders, effectively representing the difference between the account's gross buying power and net buying power.
usage: Used to answer questions like 'How much buying power would be freed if I cancelled all open orders?' or 'How much of my buying power is currently tied up in pending orders?'. This is distinct from cash commitments as it accounts for margin requirements in margin accounts.

name: Daily Rejected Orders List
description: Provides a filtered list of all orders that were rejected by the broker or exchange specifically during the current trading session, including timestamps and rejection reasons.
usage: Used when a user asks for a list of rejected orders 'today' or 'this session', distinguishing today's failures from historical rejection logs.

name: Daily Rejected Order Count
description: Calculates the total integer count of orders that were rejected during the current trading day.
usage: Used when a user needs a quick metric to answer 'Do I have any rejected orders today?' with a simple number (e.g., 0 vs 5) for a dashboard summary.

name: Rejected Order Reason Breakdown
description: Aggregates the rejected orders from the current session by the specific error code or message provided by the broker (e.g., 'Insufficient Funds', 'Symbol Not Traded', 'Exchange Closed').
usage: Used to diagnose *why* orders are being rejected, grouping them by error type to identify systemic issues like account restrictions or invalid parameters.

name: Insufficient Buying Power Rejection List
description: Filters the daily rejected orders to return only those specifically triggered by insufficient buying power, margin deficiencies, or capital constraint errors.
usage: Used to isolate which specific orders failed today due to lack of funds, distinct from rejections caused by other issues like invalid symbols or halted trading.

name: Insufficient Buying Power Rejection Notional Value
description: Calculates the total monetary value (Quantity x Price) of all orders rejected today specifically due to insufficient buying power.
usage: Used to quantify the magnitude of the buying power shortfall, answering 'How much value did I try to trade that I couldn't afford?'.

name: Insufficient Buying Power Rejection Flag
description: Returns a boolean status indicating whether any orders have been rejected today due to insufficient buying power or capital constraints.
usage: Used as a simple alert trigger or dashboard indicator to instantly notify the user if capital issues have blocked trading activity.

name: Daily Replaced/Modified Orders List
description: Identifies and lists all orders where a 'Cancel-Replace' or modification action was executed during the current trading session, distinguishing them from hard cancellations.
usage: Used when a user asks specifically for orders that were changed (e.g., price or quantity adjustment) rather than cancelled, or wants to review orders that were replaced today.

name: Order Modification History
description: Provides a granular audit trail of all changes made to a specific order throughout its lifecycle, including multiple modification events if they occurred.
usage: Used when a user needs to see the full progression of an order (e.g., 'Show me the history of changes for order #123'), revealing how price or quantity evolved from initial submission to final fill or cancellation.

name: Daily Cancel vs Replace Breakdown
description: Aggregates the total count of orders terminated during the session into two distinct buckets: 'Hard Cancelled' (removed without replacement) and 'Replaced' (cancelled and resubmitted with new parameters).
usage: Used to analyze trading behavior and intent, distinguishing between orders that were killed because the user changed their mind versus orders that were adjusted for price or size.

name: Order Fill Duration Analysis
description: Calculates the exact time elapsed between the order submission timestamp and the final fill timestamp for all orders executed during the session.
usage: Used to determine the specific 'time in queue' for each order, providing the raw data needed to analyze execution speed.

name: Slowest Filling Orders Ranking
description: Ranks filled orders by their duration in the market in descending order, highlighting the orders that took the longest to execute.
usage: Used when a user asks 'Which orders took the longest to fill?' or needs to identify orders that may have been stuck due to liquidity issues or price placement.

name: Average Order Fill Time
description: Calculates the mean, median, and maximum time to fill for all executed orders during the session to establish a baseline for execution speed.
usage: Used to benchmark individual order performance against the session average, helping to determine if a specific long fill time is an outlier.

name: Daily Average Slippage Calculation
description: Computes the statistical average difference between the limit price and the final execution price for all filled orders during the current trading session. It distinguishes between negative slippage (unfavorable fills) and price improvement (favorable fills).
usage: Used to directly answer questions about the average cost of execution relative to the limit price, providing a single metric for the day's trading efficiency.

name: Orders with Negative Slippage
description: Filters the list of filled orders to identify only those where the execution price was less favorable than the limit price (e.g., buying higher than the limit or selling lower than the limit).
usage: Used to isolate specific instances of execution failure or cost, helping a user identify which particular orders dragged down the average or resulted in unfavorable pricing.

name: Slippage Attribution by Symbol
description: Aggregates the total and average slippage/improvement by ticker symbol for the current trading day.
usage: Used to determine if specific stocks are consistently causing unfavorable fills (high slippage) or if the issue is isolated to particular securities within the day's trading activity.

name: Daily Filled Orders Notional Value
description: Calculates the total gross monetary value of all orders that were executed and filled during the current trading day, summing the absolute value of both buy and sell transactions.
usage: Used when a user asks for the total dollar volume traded, total notional value of trades, or the gross amount of stock traded today, distinct from cash flow which separates inflows and outflows.

name: Daily Notional Value by Symbol
description: Breaks down the total monetary value traded today by individual ticker symbol, showing the sum of buy and sell notional values per asset to identify which stocks had the highest turnover.
usage: Used when a user wants to see which specific stocks contributed the most to the day's trading volume or analyze turnover by specific holding.

name: Intraday Trade Win Rate
description: Calculates the percentage of day trades (positions opened and closed within the same session) that resulted in a profit specifically for the current trading day.
usage: Used when a user asks for their win rate on day trades, batting average for intraday positions, or the success rate of same-day round trips.

name: Intraday Win/Loss Trade Count
description: Aggregates the total number of winning and losing day trades executed during the current session, distinguishing same-day exits from multi-day position closes.
usage: Used to determine the specific breakdown of successful versus failed intraday trades for the day, providing the numerator and denominator for win rate calculations.

name: Intraday Realized P&L Summary
description: Calculates the total net profit or loss generated specifically from day trades (opened and closed today) during the current session, excluding P&L from longer-term holdings that were closed today.
usage: Used to assess the financial performance of strictly intraday trading activity, determining if the day's wins were profitable in dollar terms.

name: Daily Average Winning Trade P&L
description: Calculates the average monetary profit (realized P&L) for all closed trades that resulted in a gain during the current trading session.
usage: Used when a user asks for their average gain on winning trades, average profit per winning trade, or how much they make on a typical winning trade today.

name: Daily Average Winning Trade Percentage
description: Calculates the average percentage return for all closed trades that resulted in a gain during the current trading session.
usage: Used when a user wants to know the average return percentage of their winning trades today, distinct from the dollar value.

name: Daily Average Losing Trade P&L
description: Calculates the average monetary loss (realized P&L) for all closed trades that resulted in a loss during the current trading session.
usage: Used when a user asks for their average loss on losing trades or needs to compare average gains against average losses to assess risk-reward ratios.

name: Daily Largest Winning Trade
description: Identifies the single trade with the highest realized profit during the current session, providing the dollar amount, symbol, and execution time.
usage: Used to determine the specific 'home run' trade of the day or to understand the skew of winning trade performance (e.g., one big win vs. many small wins).

name: Daily Largest Losing Trade
description: Identifies the single trade with the highest negative realized profit (largest loss) during the current session.
usage: Used to determine the specific 'worst' trade of the day or to understand the skew of losing trade performance (e.g., one blown-out trade vs. many small losses).

name: Total Daily Losing P&L
description: Sums the total realized P&L specifically for all trades that closed with a loss during the current session.
usage: Used to calculate the aggregate monetary value of all unsuccessful trades, providing the numerator for average loss calculations or determining the total 'damage' for the day.

name: Daily Average Losing Trade Percentage
description: Calculates the average percentage return for all closed trades that resulted in a loss during the current trading session.
usage: Used when a user wants to know the average percentage drop of their losing trades today, distinct from the dollar value, to assess risk per trade.

name: Daily Profit Factor
description: Calculates the ratio of gross profit (total P&L from winning trades) to gross loss (absolute value of total P&L from losing trades) for the current trading session.
usage: Used to directly answer the question 'What is my profit factor?', providing a metric that indicates whether the total amount won exceeds the total amount lost on a gross basis.

name: Total Daily Winning P&L
description: Aggregates the total monetary value of all realized profits from closed trades that resulted in a gain during the current trading session.
usage: Used to determine the total gross profit generated by winning trades, which serves as the numerator for calculating the profit factor. This complements the existing 'Total Daily Losing P&L' component.

name: Daily Winning Trades Ranked by P&L
description: Provides a ranked list of all winning trades (closed positions with a profit) for the current trading session, sorted by realized profit in descending order.
usage: Used when a user wants to see not just the single largest winner, but the hierarchy of all winning trades for the day to identify top performers.

name: Daily Largest Winning Trade by Percentage
description: Identifies the single trade with the highest percentage return for the current session, distinct from the trade with the highest absolute dollar profit.
usage: Used when a user asks for the 'largest' winner in terms of efficiency or return on capital (percentage), rather than total dollar value.

name: Daily Largest Losing Trade by Percentage
description: Identifies the single closed trade with the highest negative percentage return (largest relative loss) during the current session, distinct from the absolute dollar value loss.
usage: Used to determine which trade had the worst performance relative to the position size, answering questions like 'Which stock did I lose the most percent on today?' or 'Was my biggest dollar loss also my biggest percentage loss?'.

name: Daily Losing Trades Ranked by P&L
description: Ranks all closed trades with a negative realized P&L for the current session, sorted by the magnitude of the loss in descending order.
usage: Used when a user wants to see the hierarchy of losing trades for the day, identifying not just the single largest loser but the sequence of worst performers to analyze if there were multiple significant losses.

name: Hourly Realized P&L Breakdown
description: Aggregates the realized profit and loss of closed trades by the hour of the day (e.g., 9:30-10:30, 10:30-11:30) during the current session.
usage: Used to determine the specific time of day when the largest losing trade (or aggregate losses) occurred, helping to identify if losses are concentrated in morning volatility or afternoon selloffs.

name: Closed Position Holding Duration
description: Calculates the time elapsed between the opening and closing execution timestamps for all closed positions during the current session.
usage: Used to analyze the holding period of the largest losing trade, answering questions like 'How long did I hold that position?' or 'Did the loss occur because I held it all day or flipped it quickly?'.

name: Average Holding Period Calculation
description: Calculates the arithmetic mean duration between the opening execution and closing execution timestamps for all positions closed within a specified historical date range (e.g., last 30 days).
usage: Used when a user asks 'What is my average holding period?', 'How long do I typically hold a trade?', or wants to quantify their trading timeline over a past period.

name: Historical Closed Trade Duration Log
description: Retrieves the specific entry date/time, exit date/time, and calculated duration for every individual position closed within a user-defined historical period.
usage: Used to provide the granular data underlying the average calculation, allowing users to audit specific trades or identify outliers that skewed the average holding period.

name: Holding Period Distribution Breakdown
description: Categorizes closed trades into distinct duration buckets (e.g., Intraday, 1-3 Days, 4-7 Days, 8-30 Days, >30 Days) over a historical timeframe to show the mix of trading frequencies.
usage: Used when a user wants to see if they are primarily day trading or swing trading, or asks 'How many of my trades did I hold for less than a week?'.

name: Median Holding Period Calculation
description: Calculates the statistical median duration (time between opening and closing execution) for all positions closed within a specified historical date range.
usage: Used when a user asks for the median holding period to determine the central tendency of trade duration, providing a measure that is less skewed by outliers than the average holding period.

name: Median Holding Period by Symbol
description: Calculates the statistical median duration of closed trades broken down by individual ticker symbol over a specified historical period.
usage: Used to analyze trading behavior per asset, identifying which specific stocks are held for the longest or shortest median duration compared to the overall portfolio.

name: Median Holding Period by Trade Outcome
description: Calculates the median duration separately for winning trades (profit) and losing trades (loss) over a specified historical period.
usage: Used to analyze if there is a behavioral difference in how long winning positions are held versus losing positions, answering questions like 'Do I hold losers longer than winners on average?' using median statistics.

name: Historical Average Opening Position Size
description: Calculates the mean notional value (Entry Price x Quantity) of all positions opened within a specified historical timeframe (e.g., last 30 days).
usage: Used when a user asks for their average position size, typical bet size, or average capital deployment per trade over a past period.

name: Average Winning vs Losing Position Size
description: Calculates and compares the average opening notional value of trades that resulted in a profit against those that resulted in a loss over a historical period.
usage: Used to analyze if the user sizes their winning trades differently from their losing trades, helping identify risk management biases (e.g., 'Do I bet bigger on losers?').

name: Historical Position Size Percentiles
description: Calculates the distribution of position sizes (entry notional value) over a historical period, providing percentiles (e.g., 25th, 50th, 75th, 90th) to understand the spread of trade sizing.
usage: Used when a user wants to understand the variance in their trade sizing beyond the simple average, such as identifying outliers or the most common trade size (median).

name: Historical Daily Trade Count Series
description: Provides a chronological record of the total number of trade executions (fills) that occurred on each trading day within a specified historical period.
usage: Used to visualize trading volume trends over time, calculate average daily trade frequency, or identify specific days with unusually high or low activity levels.

name: Historical Trade Activity Summary
description: Aggregates trade execution data over a specified historical period to provide summary statistics including total trades executed, average trades per trading day, and the maximum number of trades in a single day.
usage: Used to directly answer questions about average trading frequency or total trading volume over a specific past timeframe (e.g., last 30 days, YTD).

name: Historical Peak Trade Count Day Identification
description: Identifies the specific calendar date within a user-defined historical period (e.g., last 30 days) that recorded the highest number of executed trades (fills).
usage: Used to directly answer questions like 'On which day did I trade the most?' or 'When was my trading activity highest in the last month?'

name: Top Trading Days by Trade Count
description: Ranks the trading days within a specified historical period by the total number of executed trades, listing them in descending order.
usage: Used to view a ranked list of the most active trading days, identifying not just the single peak but the top N days (e.g., top 5) by volume of activity.

name: Historical Daily Notional Value Series
description: Provides a chronological time series of the total gross monetary value (Quantity x Price) of all executed trades for each trading day over a specified historical period.
usage: Used to analyze trends in trading turnover over time, visualize daily capital flow, or identify days with unusually high or low trading activity in dollar terms.

name: Historical Peak Notional Value Day Identification
description: Identifies the specific calendar date within a historical period that recorded the highest total gross notional value of traded securities.
usage: Used to directly answer questions like 'On which day did I trade the most value?' or 'When was my highest volume day by dollars?'.

name: Top Trading Days by Notional Value
description: Ranks the trading days within a specified historical period by the total gross notional value traded, listing them in descending order.
usage: Used to view a ranked list of the most active days by capital volume, identifying not just the single peak day but the top N days (e.g., top 5) by turnover.

name: Top Realized P&L Days Ranking
description: Ranks trading days within a specified historical period (e.g., 30 days) by their total realized profit and loss, identifying the specific dates with the highest monetary gains.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which days were my most profitable?' by providing a sorted list of the best performance days and their corresponding P&L values.

name: Peak Realized P&L Day Identification
description: Identifies the single trading day with the highest realized profit within a specified historical period, providing the exact date and the P&L amount.
usage: Used to determine the absolute best trading day in the last 30 days, answering 'What was my single most profitable day?' without returning a full ranked list.

name: Most Profitable Days Symbol Attribution
description: Provides the daily realized P&L for the top N most profitable days within a period, broken down by the specific ticker symbols that contributed to the profit on those dates.
usage: Used to answer 'Which stocks drove my profits on my best days?', providing attribution for the top profitable days identified by the ranking component.

name: Top Account Equity Growth Days Ranking
description: Ranks trading days within a historical period by the largest positive daily change in total account equity (Realized P&L + Unrealized P&L).
usage: Used when 'profitable' is interpreted as total portfolio growth rather than just trading activity, identifying days where net worth increased the most.

name: Worst Realized P&L Days Ranking
description: Ranks trading days within a specified historical period (e.g., 30 days) by their lowest realized profit and loss (highest monetary losses), identifying the specific dates with the worst performance.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which days were my least profitable?' by providing a sorted list of the days with the most significant losses and their corresponding P&L values.

name: Lowest Realized P&L Day Identification
description: Identifies the single trading day with the lowest realized profit (highest absolute loss) within a specified historical period, providing the exact date and the P&L amount.
usage: Used to determine the absolute worst trading day in the last 30 days, answering 'What was my single worst day?' without returning a full ranked list.

name: Least Profitable Days Symbol Attribution
description: Provides the daily realized P&L for the N least profitable days within a period, broken down by the specific ticker symbols that contributed to the losses on those dates.
usage: Used to answer 'Which stocks drove my losses on my worst days?', providing attribution for the least profitable days identified by the ranking component.

name: Daily P&L Distribution Histogram
description: Aggregates daily realized P&L values into user-defined bins (e.g., <$500, $500-$1000, >$1000) to visualize the frequency of gain and loss magnitudes over the period.
usage: Used when a user asks for the distribution of daily P&L, specifically wanting to see how often they have 'big wins', 'small losses', or 'flat days' rather than just a chronological list.

name: Daily P&L Statistical Summary
description: Calculates descriptive statistics for the daily realized P&L over a period, including mean, median, standard deviation, minimum, maximum, and quartiles.
usage: Used to quantify the central tendency and dispersion of daily performance, answering questions like 'What is my average daily P&L?' or 'How volatile are my daily results?'

name: Daily P&L Win/Loss Day Frequency
description: Counts the number of trading days that resulted in a net positive realized P&L versus a net negative realized P&L, ignoring the magnitude of the gains or losses.
usage: Used to determine the 'win rate' of trading days (e.g., 'I was profitable on 60% of days last month'), which is distinct from the win rate of individual trades.

name: Daily P&L Distribution Shape Analysis
description: Calculates the skewness (asymmetry) and kurtosis (tail heaviness) of the daily P&L distribution to describe the shape of the return profile.
usage: Used to determine if daily returns are normally distributed, prone to extreme positive outliers (fat right tail), or prone to crash days (fat left tail).

name: Standard Deviation of Daily Equity Change
description: Calculates the statistical standard deviation of the daily absolute dollar change in total account equity (combining realized and unrealized P&L) over a specified historical period.
usage: Used to quantify the daily volatility of total account value fluctuations in currency terms, answering questions about the variability of net worth changes distinct from percentage-based return volatility.

name: Portfolio Fixed Period Total Return
description: Calculates the total percentage gain or loss and absolute dollar change for the entire portfolio (equity + cash) over a defined historical window of trading days (e.g., 30 days).
usage: Used when a user asks for the total portfolio return over a specific recent period (e.g., 'last 30 trading days'), 'how much did my portfolio grow this month', or needs the aggregated performance metric for the portfolio distinct from individual position returns.

name: Cumulative Realized P&L Time Series
description: Provides a running total of daily realized P&L over a specified historical period, aggregating the daily trading results to show the accumulation of profits or losses.
usage: Used to visualize the buildup of trading P&L over time (e.g., a cumulative sum of the daily realized P&L series), answering questions about the trajectory of trading performance rather than just the final total.

name: Rolling Window Portfolio Return
description: Calculates the cumulative total return of the portfolio over a rolling window (e.g., 20 or 30 trading days) across a historical timeframe, returning a time series of rolling returns.
usage: Used to analyze how the portfolio's performance over a specific lookback window (like 30 days) has trended over the last year, identifying cycles of strong or weak performance.

name: Recent 20-Day Moving Average Crossover Identification
description: Identifies open positions where the price crossed above (bullish) or below (bearish) the 20-day moving average within a specified historical lookback window (e.g., last 3 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks for stocks that recently crossed their 20-day MA, broke short-term trend lines, or experienced a recent 20-day crossover event.

name: Last 20-Day Moving Average Crossover Date
description: Provides the most recent date on which the price crossed the 20-day moving average for each open position, regardless of how long ago it occurred, along with the direction of the cross.
usage: Used to determine how long a position has been in its current short-term trend state relative to the 20-day MA, or to identify the date of the most recent trend change.

name: Recent 20-Day Moving Average Bearish Crossover Filter
description: Identifies and lists open positions where the price specifically crossed *below* the 20-day moving average within a user-defined lookback window (e.g., last 3 trading days), excluding bullish crossovers.
usage: Used when a user asks specifically for positions that crossed below the 20-day MA, distinguishing them from stocks that crossed above, or wants a filtered list of short-term breakdowns.

name: 20-Day MA Crossover Volume Confirmation
description: Compares the trading volume on the specific day of the 20-day moving average crossover against the stock's average volume to determine if the price breakdown was supported by institutional selling pressure.
usage: Used to validate the significance of a bearish crossover signal; a cross down on high volume is considered a stronger, more valid breakdown signal than one on low volume.

name: 20-Day MA Bearish Crossover Post-Event Return
description: Calculates the percentage return of a position starting from the confirmed date of the most recent bearish 20-day moving average crossover to the current moment.
usage: Used to determine if a bearish crossover signal was 'correct' or profitable in the short term, answering questions like 'Did the stock actually keep falling after it crossed the 20-day line?'

name: 20-Day MA Crossover Close Confirmation
description: Determines if the price closed below the 20-day moving average on the crossover day versus merely crossing intraday before recovering, filtering for 'fake-out' signals.
usage: Used to distinguish between confirmed breakdowns (Close < MA) and intraday probes (Intraday Low < MA but Close > MA), helping traders assess signal reliability.

name: Recent 50-Day Moving Average Crossover Identification
description: Identifies open positions where the price crossed the 50-day moving average (either direction) within a specific historical lookback window (e.g., last 5 trading days).
usage: Used when a user asks for stocks that recently crossed their 50-day MA, broke medium-term trend lines, or experienced a recent 50-day crossover event.

name: Recent 50-Day Moving Average Bullish Crossover Filter
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the price specifically crossed above the 50-day moving average within a user-defined lookback window.
usage: Used when a user asks specifically for positions that crossed above the 50-day MA, distinguishing them from stocks that crossed below, or wants a filtered list of medium-term bullish breakouts.

name: Last 50-Day Moving Average Crossover Date
description: Provides the most recent date on which the price crossed the 50-day moving average for each open position, regardless of how long ago it occurred, along with the direction of the cross.
usage: Used to determine how long a position has been in its current medium-term trend state relative to the 50-day MA, or to identify the date of the most recent trend change.

name: 50-Day MA Crossover Volume Confirmation
description: Compares the trading volume on the specific day of the 50-day moving average crossover against the stock's average volume to determine if the price breakout was supported by institutional buying pressure.
usage: Used to validate the significance of a bullish crossover signal; a cross up on high volume is considered a stronger, more valid breakout signal than one on low volume.

name: Recent 50-Day Moving Average Bearish Crossover Filter
description: Identifies and lists open positions where the price specifically crossed below the 50-day moving average within a user-defined lookback window (e.g., last 5 trading days), excluding bullish crossovers.
usage: Used when a user asks specifically for positions that crossed below the 50-day MA, distinguishing them from stocks that crossed above, or wants a filtered list of medium-term breakdowns.

name: Symbol High Volume Up Day Log
description: Identifies specific dates within a historical lookback period where a security's price closed higher than the previous close *and* the trading volume exceeded a defined benchmark (e.g., 30-day average) by a specified multiplier.
usage: Used to isolate individual accumulation or momentum days where volume confirms the price move, serving as the raw data for streak analysis or event studies.

name: Symbol Consecutive High Volume Up Day Streaks
description: Analyzes historical price and volume data to identify streaks of consecutive days where the price closed up with above-average volume, calculating the start date, end date, and cumulative return of each streak.
usage: Used to determine the duration and performance of sustained accumulation patterns for a specific stock, answering questions about the strength of recent momentum runs.

name: Position Consecutive High Volume Up Day Filter
description: Filters the portfolio's open positions to identify only those securities that have experienced a streak of consecutive 'high volume up days' of a minimum specified length within the recent trading days.
usage: Used to screen a portfolio for active momentum setups, specifically answering questions like 'Which positions had two consecutive high-volume up days recently?' or finding stocks under active accumulation.

name: Symbol High Volume Down Day Log
description: Identifies specific dates within a historical lookback period where a security's price closed lower than the previous close and the trading volume exceeded a defined benchmark (e.g., 30-day average) by a specified multiplier.
usage: Used to isolate individual distribution or capitulation days where volume confirms the price drop, serving as the raw data for identifying high-volume down streaks.

name: Symbol Consecutive High Volume Down Day Streaks
description: Analyzes historical price and volume data to identify streaks of consecutive days where the price closed down with above-average volume, calculating the start date, end date, and cumulative decline of each streak.
usage: Used to determine the duration and severity of sustained distribution patterns for a specific stock, answering questions about the strength of recent sell-offs.

name: Position Consecutive High Volume Down Day Filter
description: Filters the portfolio's open positions to identify only those securities that have experienced a streak of consecutive 'high volume down days' of a minimum specified length (e.g., 2) within a recent user-defined lookback window (e.g., last 10 trading days).
usage: Used to screen a portfolio for active distribution setups or capitulation events, specifically answering questions like 'Which positions had two consecutive high-volume down days recently?'.

name: Position Daily Range Contraction Trend
description: Calculates the linear regression slope of the daily intraday range (High - Low) for each open position over a specified lookback period (e.g., 10 days) to determine if volatility is compressing.
usage: Used to identify positions experiencing a 'squeeze' or consolidation where price action is becoming tighter, often a precursor to a breakout.

name: Position Short vs. Long Term Range Ratio
description: Compares the average daily intraday range of a short-term window (e.g., 10 days) to a longer-term window (e.g., 30 or 60 days) to quantify recent compression relative to historical norms.
usage: Used to identify positions where current volatility is significantly lower than recent history, highlighting consolidation setups.

name: Consecutive Lower Range Days Filter
description: Counts the number of consecutive trading days within the last 10 sessions where the daily intraday range was smaller than the previous day's range.
usage: Used to find positions that are actively tightening on a day-by-day basis, indicating a continuous reduction in volatility.

name: Position Daily Range Expansion Trend
description: Calculates the linear regression slope of the daily intraday range (High - Low) for each open position over the last 10 trading days to determine if volatility is expanding.
usage: Used when a user asks for positions with expanding daily ranges, increasing volatility, or widening trading bands over a specific short-term period.

name: 50-Day MA Reversion from 5% Deviation
description: Identifies open positions where the price has returned to (crossed or touched) the 50-day moving average after previously deviating by at least 5% above or below that average within a specified lookback period.
usage: Used to screen for stocks that have successfully reverted to the mean after a significant overextension, answering the specific question of which positions returned after a defined move.

name: Historical 50-Day MA Deviation Event Log
description: Provides a chronological list of dates when a position's price deviated from its 50-day moving average by a user-defined percentage threshold (e.g., 5%), including the specific percentage deviation and direction.
usage: Used to verify the historical deviation events that qualify a stock for the reversion criteria, ensuring the 5% move actually occurred before the return.

name: Positions Within 20-Day MA Proximity
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities where the current market price is within a specific user-defined percentage threshold (e.g., 1%) of the 20-day moving average.
usage: Used when a user wants to identify stocks trading near their short-term trend line, finding consolidation setups or support/resistance touches, specifically answering questions like 'Which stocks are within 1% of the 20-day MA?'.

name: 50-Day MA Absolute Deviation Filter
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the absolute percentage difference between the current market price and the 50-day moving average exceeds a user-defined threshold.
