usage: Used to identify stocks that are significantly overextended in either direction (overbought or oversold) from their medium-term trend, answering questions like 'Which positions are trading more than 5% away from their 50-day MA?'

name: Positions Significantly Above 50-Day MA
description: Identifies open positions where the current price is above the 50-day moving average by a user-defined percentage threshold, highlighting potential overbought conditions or strong breakouts.
usage: Used when a user specifically asks for positions that are extended to the upside (e.g., 'Which stocks are 5% above their 50-day MA?'), complementing the existing library's filter for downside deviations.

name: Top Positions by 50-Day MA Deviation
description: Ranks open positions by the magnitude of their percentage deviation from the 50-day moving average, identifying the securities with the largest gap between current price and the average.
usage: Used to determine which positions are the most extended from their medium-term trend line, regardless of whether they are trading above or below it.

name: 200-Day MA Absolute Deviation Filter
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the absolute percentage difference between the current market price and the 200-day moving average exceeds a user-defined threshold.
usage: Used to identify positions that are significantly overextended in either direction (overbought or oversold) relative to the long-term 200-day trend line, specifically answering questions like 'Which positions are more than X% away from their 200-day MA?'.

name: Top Positions by 200-Day MA Deviation
description: Ranks open positions by the magnitude of their percentage deviation from the 200-day moving average, identifying the securities with the largest gap between current price and the long-term average.
usage: Used to determine which positions are the most extended from their long-term trend line, regardless of whether they are trading above or below it. This complements the 50-day deviation ranking component already in the library.

name: Position 30-Day Moving Average Slope
description: Calculates the linear regression slope or rate of change of the 30-day simple moving average for each open position, quantifying the velocity of the trend over the specific period requested.
usage: Used to determine the strength and direction of the price trend specifically over the last 30 trading days, identifying which positions have the steepest positive momentum slopes.

name: Position Level MACD
description: Calculates the Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) indicator values—the MACD line (12-period EMA minus 26-period EMA), the Signal line (9-period EMA of MACD), and the Histogram (MACD minus Signal)—for each open position.
usage: Used when a user asks for momentum indicators, specifically MACD, to identify trend strength and potential reversals based on moving average convergence and divergence.

name: Top Positions by MACD Histogram
description: Ranks open positions by their MACD Histogram value in descending order, highlighting the securities where the short-term momentum is accelerating the most relative to the signal line.
usage: Used to identify which positions currently have the strongest bullish momentum acceleration, complementing raw return data with oscillator-based momentum strength.

name: Position Average Directional Index (ADX)
description: Calculates the Average Directional Index (ADX) for each open position to quantify the strength of the trend regardless of direction (bullish or bearish), with higher values indicating stronger momentum.
usage: Used to distinguish between strong trending markets (high ADX) and ranging markets (low ADX), allowing the user to identify which positions are exhibiting the most significant trend intensity.

name: Position Level 30-Day Rate of Change (ROC)
description: Calculates the percentage change in price between the current day and the price 30 trading days ago for each open position, providing a specific momentum metric.
usage: Used to quantify the raw momentum of a position over the specific 30-day timeframe requested, distinguishing it from generic period returns.

name: Lowest Performing Positions by Fixed Period Return
description: Ranks open positions by their lowest total percentage return over a specified historical period (e.g., 30 trading days), highlighting the weakest or most negative momentum performers.
usage: Used to directly identify which positions have the lowest momentum or are the biggest losers over a specific fixed timeframe.

name: Position 30-Day Momentum Percentile Rank
description: Determines the percentile rank of each position's 30-day return relative to all other open positions in the portfolio, identifying the bottom-tier momentum stocks.
usage: Used to understand how a specific position's 30-day performance compares to the rest of the portfolio, specifically identifying those in the lowest percentiles (e.g., bottom 10%).

name: Top Positions by 30-Day Return Volatility
description: Calculates the annualized standard deviation of daily returns over the last 30 trading days for each open position and ranks them in descending order to identify the most volatile holdings.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions are the most volatile, has the highest risk, or moves the most in percentage terms over the last month.

name: Lowest Positions by 30-Day Return Volatility
description: Calculates the annualized standard deviation of daily returns over the last 30 trading days for each open position and ranks them in ascending order to identify the least volatile holdings.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions are the least volatile, most stable, or acts as a 'defensive' holding within the portfolio.

name: Positions Exceeding 30-Day Volatility Threshold
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the 30-day annualized volatility exceeds a specific user-defined percentage (e.g., > 40%).
usage: Used to screen for extremely volatile or risky assets within the portfolio based on a specific risk tolerance level.

name: Positions Below 30-Day Volatility Threshold
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the 30-day annualized volatility is less than a specific user-defined percentage threshold.
usage: Used when a user wants to screen for 'quiet' stocks under a specific risk level (e.g., 'Show me positions with vol under 15%') rather than just viewing a ranked list of the lowest.

name: Position 30-Day Volatility vs Portfolio Average
description: Calculates the difference or ratio between each position's individual 30-day volatility and the aggregate portfolio's average 30-day volatility.
usage: Used to determine which positions are significantly safer or riskier than the typical holding in the user's specific portfolio context, identifying relative defensive assets.

name: Position 30-Day Volatility vs Benchmark
description: Compares the 30-day realized volatility of each open position against the 30-day realized volatility of a specified benchmark index (e.g., SPY or QQQ).
usage: Used to determine if a position's 'low' volatility is due to a quiet market environment or if the stock is inherently less volatile than the broader market.

name: Top Positions by 20-Day Average Intraday Range
description: Calculates the arithmetic mean of the daily intraday range (High - Low) for each open position over the last 20 trading days and ranks them from highest to lowest.
usage: Used when a user asks to identify the most volatile positions in their portfolio over the last month based on daily price width, specifically requesting a ranking of the 20-day average.

name: Lowest Positions by 20-Day Average Intraday Range
description: Calculates the arithmetic mean of the daily intraday range (High - Low) for each open position over the last 20 trading days and ranks them in ascending order to identify the least volatile holdings.
usage: Used to identify the tightest trading ranges or most stagnant positions over the last month, providing the inverse view of the highest average intraday range ranking.

name: Position 20-Day Average Intraday Range Breakdown
description: Provides the average daily intraday range (High - Low) for every open position over the last 20 trading days without applying a ranking sort, returning the raw list with metrics.
usage: Used when a user wants to see the specific average range value for all positions individually, perhaps to perform custom filtering or manual review, rather than just viewing the top or bottom ranked lists.

name: Position Trading Halt Frequency Ranking
description: Ranks open positions by the total number of trading suspension or halt events that occurred within a specified historical lookback period (e.g., 30 trading days).
usage: Used to directly identify the stocks in the portfolio with the highest frequency of trading halts, answering the specific question about which positions are most prone to trading stoppages.

name: Position Trading Halt Event Log
description: Retrieves a detailed chronological list of trading halt events for open positions within a specified period, including the start time, end time, duration, and the specific exchange halt code (e.g., 'T1', 'LUDP').
usage: Used to analyze the specific nature and duration of trading halts, distinguishing between brief volatility pauses (Limit Up-Limit Down) and news-related suspensions.

name: Position Total Halt Duration
description: Aggregates the total cumulative time that trading was halted for each open position over a specific period, summing the duration of all individual events.
usage: Used to assess the impact of halts not just by frequency (count) but by total time the stock was untradeable, which may affect liquidity risk more than just the number of pauses.

name: Position Linear Regression R-Squared
description: Calculates the R-squared (coefficient of determination) of a linear regression fit to the daily closing prices over the last 30 trading days.
usage: Used when a user wants to identify positions with the most 'stable' or predictable trend lines. A high R-squared indicates the price is moving smoothly in a consistent direction (stable trend), while a low R-squared indicates erratic or choppy price action.

name: Lowest Positions by ADX
description: Ranks open positions by their Average Directional Index (ADX) in ascending order to identify the least directional stocks.
usage: Used to find positions that are 'stable' in the sense of being in a consolidation phase or trading sideways (low trend strength), as opposed to trending stocks which have higher ADX values.

name: Position Daily Closing Price Standard Deviation
description: Calculates the standard deviation of the absolute daily closing prices (not percentage returns) over the last 30 trading days.
usage: Used when a user wants to measure stability in terms of absolute price points rather than percentage moves. This identifies stocks whose closing prices fluctuate within the tightest dollar range, regardless of the stock's share price.

name: Position 15-Day Consecutive Price Streaks
description: Calculates the maximum streak length of consecutive days where the closing price was higher (up streak) or lower (down streak) than the previous day for each open position within the trailing 15-day window.
usage: Used to analyze short-term momentum behavior of current holdings, identifying the strongest runs of gains or losses over the last three weeks.

name: Top Positions by 15-Day Consecutive Up Closes
description: Ranks the portfolio's open positions by the length of their longest streak of consecutive higher closes over the last 15 trading days, highlighting the stocks with the most persistent upward momentum.
usage: Directly answers which holdings exhibited the strongest winning streaks recently by providing a sorted list of positions by their consecutive up-close counts.

name: Top Positions by 15-Day Consecutive Down Closes
description: Ranks the portfolio's open positions by the length of their longest streak of consecutive lower closes over the last 15 trading days, highlighting the stocks with the most persistent negative momentum.
usage: Directly answers which holdings exhibited the strongest losing streaks recently by providing a sorted list of positions by their consecutive down-close counts within the 15-day window.

name: Positions Ranked by Earliest Bullish Crossover Date
description: Ranks open positions by the specific date of their most recent bullish crossover event (e.g., Golden Cross or Price crossing above an MA) that occurred within a defined historical window (e.g., last 30 days), sorting from the earliest date to the latest.
usage: Used when a user asks which positions had the earliest crossover, identifying the stocks that turned bullish first during a specific rally period.

name: Custom Period Moving Average Crossover Date Lookup
description: Calculates the specific date on which a short-term moving average crossed above a long-term moving average (bullish signal) for each open position within a user-defined lookback period.
usage: Used to find the exact date of a bullish crossover for specific moving average pairs (e.g., 20-day crossing 50-day, or 10-day crossing 30-day) that are not hardcoded in the standard Golden Cross component.

name: Custom Period Price vs MA Crossover Date Lookup
description: Identifies the specific date within a lookback period when the price of an open position crossed above a user-defined moving average (e.g., Price > 20-day SMA).
usage: Used to determine when a stock broke out above a custom short or medium-term trend line, complementing the existing 50-day specific component.

name: Positions Ranked by Earliest Bearish Crossover Date
description: Identifies open positions that experienced a bearish moving average crossover event (e.g., Death Cross, Price crossing below 50-day MA, or 50-day MA crossing below 200-day MA) within a specific historical window and ranks them by the date of the event from earliest to latest.
usage: Used to determine which positions turned bearish first within a given period, answering questions like 'Which position has been in a downtrend the longest?' or 'Which stock broke its trend line earliest this month?'.

name: 20-Day vs 50-Day Moving Average Slope Comparison
description: Calculates the slope (rate of change) of the 20-day moving average and the 50-day moving average for each open position and determines the mathematical difference between them.
usage: Used to identify positions where short-term price velocity (20-day slope) is diverging from medium-term velocity (50-day slope), indicating acceleration or deceleration in the trend.

name: Top Positions by 20-Day vs 50-Day Slope Differential
description: Ranks open positions by the largest positive difference between the 20-day moving average slope and the 50-day moving average slope.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions have improving 20-day vs 50-day momentum?' by identifying stocks where short-term trend velocity is significantly stronger than medium-term trend velocity.

name: 20-Day Moving Average Slope Acceleration
description: Calculates the Rate of Change (ROC) of the 20-day moving average slope over a short window (e.g., 5 days) to determine if the short-term trend is accelerating or decelerating.
usage: Used to refine 'improving momentum' by measuring if the slope itself is getting steeper, not just if it is positive relative to the 50-day slope.

name: Positions with Positive Slope Divergence
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the 20-day moving average slope is positive and exceeds the 50-day moving average slope.
usage: Used to isolate positions exhibiting 'golden' momentum characteristics—where the short-term trend is not only up but accelerating faster than the medium-term trend.

name: Positions with Negative Slope Divergence
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the 20-day moving average slope is less than the 50-day moving average slope, indicating that short-term momentum is deteriorating relative to the medium-term trend.
usage: Used when a user asks for positions with slowing momentum, flattening trends, or specifically where the 20-day slope is weaker than the 50-day slope.

name: Worst Positions by 20-Day vs 50-Day Slope Differential
description: Ranks open positions by the most negative difference between the 20-day moving average slope and the 50-day moving average slope, identifying the positions with the greatest loss of short-term momentum compared to the long-term trend.
usage: Used to identify the positions with the most significant deterioration in momentum slopes, ranking them by the severity of the negative divergence.

name: Position 10-Day Average True Range
description: Calculates the Average True Range (ATR) for each open position using a 10-period lookback, providing a short-term volatility metric distinct from the standard 14-day ATR.
usage: Used to establish the baseline volatility level for the specific 10-day timeframe requested, serving as the foundation for trend analysis.

name: Position ATR 10-Day Rate of Change
description: Calculates the percentage difference between the current 10-day Average True Range and the 10-day ATR from 10 trading days prior to determine the direction and magnitude of volatility expansion or contraction.
usage: Used to quantify exactly how much volatility has increased or decreased over the last 10 days, answering the 'increased' part of the user's question.

name: Positions with Increasing 10-Day ATR
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities where the 10-day Average True Range has increased (positive rate of change) over the specified period.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions have increased average true range?' by isolating the list of assets experiencing short-term volatility expansion.

name: Position ATR 10-Day Slope
description: Calculates the linear regression slope of the Average True Range values over the last 10 trading days to determine the consistency and direction of the volatility trend.
usage: Used to distinguish between positions where volatility is steadily ramping up (positive slope) versus those experiencing a sudden spike, providing context on the nature of the increase.

name: Positions with Decreasing 10-Day ATR
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities where the 10-day Average True Range (ATR) has declined (negative rate of change) compared to 10 trading days prior.
usage: Used to directly answer questions about which specific holdings are experiencing reduced volatility or a contraction in daily price range, identifying potential squeeze setups or consolidating assets.

name: Top Positions by 10-Day ATR Contraction
description: Ranks open positions by the magnitude of the decline in their 10-day Average True Range, listing the securities with the largest negative percentage change in volatility.
usage: Used to identify which positions are experiencing the most significant drop in volatility (e.g., 'Which positions are quieting down the most?'), useful for finding tight ranges or preparing for potential breakouts.

name: Significant 10-Day ATR Decrease Filter
description: Identifies open positions where the 10-day Average True Range has decreased by a user-defined percentage threshold (e.g., > 20% decrease), filtering out minor fluctuations.
usage: Used when a user wants to find only the positions that have seen a material drop in volatility, distinguishing meaningful contraction from noise.

name: Position 5-Day Relative Volume Trend Slope
description: Calculates the linear regression slope of the Relative Volume ratio (Current Daily Volume / Historical Average Volume) over the last 5 trading days for each open position.
usage: Used to mathematically quantify the trend of volume activity relative to a stock's own history, distinguishing between an absolute increase in shares traded and an increase in trading intensity relative to the norm.

name: Positions with Increasing 5-Day Relative Volume
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities where the 5-day Relative Volume trend slope is positive, indicating that volume is accelerating relative to its historical average.
usage: Used to directly answer the question 'Which positions have increasing relative volume?' by isolating stocks experiencing rising institutional or retail interest compared to their baseline liquidity.

name: Position 5-Day Relative Volume Time Series
description: Provides the daily Relative Volume ratio for each of the last 5 trading days for every open position, allowing for visual inspection of the volume trend.
usage: Used to audit the slope calculation or visualize the specific trajectory of relative volume (e.g., 1.0x, 1.2x, 1.5x) to confirm the 'increasing' nature of the activity.

name: Positions with Decreasing 5-Day Relative Volume
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the 5-day linear regression slope of relative volume is negative, indicating declining trading interest relative to historical norms.
usage: Used to directly answer which positions are losing volume momentum or seeing reduced trading activity relative to their average over the last week.

name: Top Positions by 5-Day Relative Volume Decline
description: Ranks open positions by the magnitude of the negative 5-day relative volume trend slope, identifying the securities experiencing the most rapid drop in trading interest.
usage: Used to identify which positions have the fastest shrinking trading volume relative to their historical average, highlighting stocks where liquidity is drying up the fastest.

name: Top Positions by Max Relative Volume (vs 20-Day Avg) Last 10 Days
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by the highest single-day relative volume ratio (Daily Volume / 20-Day Average Volume) observed within the last 10 trading days.
usage: Used to identify which positions experienced the most significant volume spikes relative to their baseline liquidity, answering the question of which stocks had the highest surge in trading activity compared to their average.

name: Position Volume Spike Event Log (vs 20-Day Avg) Last 10 Days
description: Provides a chronological list of dates within the last 10 trading days where a position's daily volume exceeded a user-defined threshold multiple of its 20-day average volume (e.g., > 2x).
usage: Used to analyze the frequency and timing of volume surges for each holding, identifying if a spike was a one-off event or part of a pattern of accumulation or distribution.

name: Top Positions by Downside Gap vs ATR Ratio
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by the magnitude of their most significant downside gap normalized by the Average True Range (ATR) over a specific lookback period (e.g., 20 trading days). This metric quantifies the 'shock' of the gap relative to the stock's recent volatility.
usage: Used to answer 'Which positions had the largest downside gap relative to ATR?' by finding the highest ratio of (Gap / ATR) for downside gaps.

name: Position Downside Gap vs ATR Event Log
description: Provides a chronological list of all downside gap events for open positions within a specified lookback period, calculating the gap size and expressing it as a multiple of the Average True Range (ATR).
usage: Used to review the specific dates, gap sizes, and volatility-adjusted ratios for all negative opening gaps, allowing for detailed analysis beyond just the single largest event.

name: Position Max Downside Gap vs ATR Calculation
description: Calculates the specific value of the largest downside gap expressed as a multiple of ATR for each individual open position without applying a ranking sort.
usage: Used to retrieve the raw 'Gap vs ATR' metric for all holdings, enabling users to perform custom filtering (e.g., 'Show me positions where the gap was > 2x ATR') or independent analysis.

name: Top Positions by Upside Gap vs ATR Ratio
description: Identifies and ranks open positions by the highest ratio of upside gap magnitude (Opening Price minus Previous Close) to their Average True Range (ATR) over the last 20 trading days.
usage: Used to identify which positions experienced the most significant bullish opening moves relative to their recent volatility, answering the specific question about the largest upside gap vs ATR.

name: Position Max Upside Gap vs ATR Calculation
description: Calculates the specific value of the largest upside gap expressed as a multiple of ATR for each individual open position within the last 20 trading days.
usage: Used to retrieve the raw 'Gap vs ATR' metric for all holdings without applying a ranking sort, enabling users to perform custom filtering or analysis on the magnitude of bullish opening shocks.

name: Position Upside Gap vs ATR Event Log
description: Provides a chronological list of upside gap events for open positions within the last 20 trading days, calculating the gap size and expressing it as a multiple of the Average True Range (ATR).
usage: Used to analyze the frequency and timing of significant upside opening gaps relative to volatility, allowing users to see if a high ratio was a one-off event or part of a pattern.

name: Position Level Bollinger Bands
description: Calculates the Upper, Middle (20-day SMA), and Lower Bollinger Bands for each open position based on standard deviation (typically 2 SD) over a 20-day lookback period.
usage: Used when a user asks for the current Bollinger Band values of their holdings to assess if a stock is overextended or trading within normal volatility ranges.

name: Positions Closing Above Upper Bollinger Band
description: Identifies and lists open positions where the daily closing price exceeded the Upper Bollinger Band within a specific historical lookback window (e.g., last 10 trading days).
usage: Used to answer the specific question about which positions broke above the upper band recently, helping to identify overbought conditions or breakouts.

name: Bollinger Band Penetration Event Log
description: Provides a chronological list of all dates within a specified period where an open position's closing price moved outside (above upper or below lower) the Bollinger Bands.
usage: Used to analyze the frequency and timing of volatility breakouts or breakdowns, providing a detailed history of when positions left their standard deviation range.

name: Positions Closing Below Lower Bollinger Band
description: Identifies and lists open positions where the daily closing price fell below the Lower Bollinger Band within a specific historical lookback window (e.g., last 10 trading days).
usage: Used to find positions that are statistically oversold or experiencing capitulation, serving as the inverse component to the upper-band filter.

name: Top Positions by Lower Bollinger Band Breach Severity
description: Calculates the percentage difference between the closing price and the Lower Bollinger Band for each open position on the specific day(s) they breached the band within the lookback window, ranking them by the magnitude of the breach.
usage: Used to identify which positions were the most statistically oversold or experienced the most significant breakdown below the lower band, rather than just listing all positions that breached.

name: Consecutive Days Below Lower Bollinger Band
description: Calculates the current streak count of consecutive trading days where a position has closed below its Lower Bollinger Band.
usage: Used to distinguish between temporary one-day dips below the band versus persistent oversold conditions where the price remains 'outside' the bands for multiple days.

name: Top Positions by Narrowest Bollinger Band Width
description: Calculates the difference between the Upper and Lower Bollinger Bands for each open position and ranks them in ascending order to identify the tightest trading ranges.
usage: Used when a user asks for the positions with the smallest band width, tightest Bollinger Bands, or which stocks are currently experiencing the lowest volatility relative to the Bollinger Bands indicator.

name: Position Bollinger Band Width Percentile
description: Determines the percentile rank of the current Bollinger Band width relative to the stock's own historical width distribution over a specified long-term period (e.g., 1 year).
usage: Used to identify Bollinger Band 'Squeezes' (e.g., width in the bottom 5% or 10% of history), distinguishing between stocks that are naturally low-volatility and those undergoing a significant volatility contraction.

name: Position Bollinger Band Width vs Moving Average
description: Compares the current Bollinger Band width to the average width over a user-defined lookback period (e.g., 50-day average of width) to identify if the bands are contracting or expanding relative to the recent mean.
usage: Used to determine if the current narrow width is statistically significant compared to recent norms, or to calculate the BandWidth ratio (Current Width / Average Width) commonly used in technical analysis to detect breakouts.

name: Position Bollinger Band Width Trend
description: Calculates the linear regression slope or rate of change of the Bollinger Band width over a specified short-term window (e.g., 10 or 20 days) to determine if volatility is contracting or expanding.
usage: Used to identify the trajectory of the band width, helping to determine if the 'narrowest' condition is a fresh development (recent contraction) or a stable state.

name: Top Positions by Widest Bollinger Band Width
description: Calculates the difference between the Upper and Lower Bollinger Bands for each open position and ranks them in descending order to identify the securities with the largest trading ranges and highest volatility.
usage: Used to directly answer questions about which positions have the widest Bollinger Bands, identifying high-volatility assets or those experiencing significant price expansion.

name: Position All-Time High Proximity
description: Calculates the specific distance (percentage and absolute) between the current market price and the security's all-time historical high price.
usage: Used when a user wants to know how close a position is to its lifetime peak, which is a significant resistance level and a major potential breakout point.

name: Positions Coiling Near Recent Highs
description: Identifies positions where the price is within a user-defined percentage threshold of a recent high (e.g., 20-day or 52-week high) AND the price volatility (ATR or Bollinger Band Width) is contracting below its historical average.
usage: Used to find high-probability breakout setups where price is compressing or 'coiling' at resistance levels, indicating stored energy for a potential explosive upward move.

name: Position Custom Lookback High Proximity
description: Calculates the highest price reached within a user-defined historical window (e.g., 30 days, 6 months) and the current percentage distance from that level for each open position.
usage: Used to assess breakout potential against flexible recent resistance levels (e.g., 3-month highs) rather than the fixed 52-week period, allowing for more granular analysis of intermediate-term trends.

name: Position 20-Day Z-Score Calculation
description: Calculates the statistical Z-score for each open position, representing the number of standard deviations the current price is from its 20-day moving average.
usage: Used to identify statistically extreme price movements. A high positive Z-score indicates overextension to the upside (potential short/mean reversion down), while a high negative Z-score indicates overextension to the downside (potential long/mean reversion up).

name: Top Positions by Lowest 20-Day Z-Score
description: Ranks open positions by their Z-score in ascending order (most negative first), highlighting the securities that are the most statistically oversold relative to their 20-day mean.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions show the strongest mean reversion potential?' by identifying the assets that have deviated furthest below their statistical mean, presenting the highest probability of a price rebound.

name: Top Positions by Highest 20-Day Z-Score
description: Ranks open positions by their Z-score in descending order (most positive first), highlighting the securities that are the most statistically overbought relative to their 20-day mean.
usage: Used to identify positions that are extended far above their statistical mean, presenting potential mean reversion opportunities to the downside (shorting or taking profits).

name: Top Positions by Absolute 20-Day Z-Score
description: Ranks open positions by the absolute value of their Z-score, identifying the securities with the most extreme price deviation regardless of direction (up or down).
usage: Used to find the positions exhibiting the highest volatility or abnormal behavior compared to their recent 20-day history, filtering out direction to focus purely on statistical extremity.

name: Position 20-Day Bearish Intraday Reversal Count
description: Calculates the number of trading days within the last 20 sessions where the stock opened higher than it closed (Open > Close), indicating selling pressure took over after the open.
usage: Used to identify stocks that frequently open strong and sell off during the session (bull trap patterns), which can be a sign of distribution or choppiness.

name: Top Positions by 20-Day Bearish Intraday Reversal Frequency
description: Ranks open positions by the frequency of bearish intraday reversals (days where Open > Close) over the last 20 trading days.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions had the highest frequency of opening strong and closing weak?', helping identify the most choppy or distribution-heavy assets.

name: Position 20-Day Bullish Intraday Reversal Count
description: Calculates the number of trading days within the last 20 sessions where the stock opened lower than it closed (Open < Close), indicating buying pressure took over after the open.
usage: Used to identify stocks that frequently dip at the open and recover throughout the session (bear trap patterns), showing intraday resilience or accumulation.

name: Top Positions by 20-Day Bullish Intraday Reversal Frequency
description: Ranks open positions by the frequency of bullish intraday reversals (days where Open < Close) over the last 20 trading days.
usage: Used to find positions that consistently recover from early weakness, highlighting opportunities for dip-buying strategies or identifying strong intraday demand.

name: Lowest Positions by 20-Day Bearish Intraday Reversal Frequency
description: Ranks open positions by the lowest frequency of days where the stock opened higher than it closed over the last 20 trading days.
usage: Used to identify stocks that rarely exhibit 'bull trap' behavior or fade from their opening price, indicating potential sustained momentum from the open or a lack of early selling pressure.

name: Lowest Positions by 20-Day Bullish Intraday Reversal Frequency
description: Ranks open positions by the lowest frequency of days where the stock opened lower than it closed over the last 20 trading days.
usage: Used to identify stocks that rarely recover from early weakness or exhibit 'bear trap' behavior, indicating sustained selling pressure from the open or a lack of intraday dip-buying interest.

name: Position Gap & Fill Frequency Count
description: Calculates the total number of trading days within the last 30 sessions where a position opened with a gap (up or down) and subsequently traded back to the previous session's closing price within the same day.
usage: Used to quantify the occurrence of gap-and-fill patterns for specific holdings, providing the data needed to identify which stocks most frequently exhibit this intraday mean-reversion behavior.

name: Top Positions by Gap & Fill Frequency
description: Ranks open positions by the total number of times they experienced a gap and subsequent intraday fill over the last 30 trading days, highlighting the securities with the highest frequency of this pattern.
usage: Used to directly answer which positions most frequently gapped and then filled the gap, identifying the stocks most prone to this specific intraday volatility pattern.

name: Position Gap & Fill Event Log
description: Provides a chronological list of specific dates within the last 30 trading days where a position opened with a gap and subsequently filled the gap intraday, including gap size and direction.
usage: Used to audit the frequency count by identifying the exact days the gap-and-fill pattern occurred, helping to verify the data and analyze the context (e.g., earnings, news) of each event.

name: Position Average Opening Hour Return
description: Calculates the average percentage price return during the first hour of the trading session (e.g., 9:30 AM to 10:30 AM) over the last 20 trading days for each open position.
usage: Used to determine which open positions have historically shown strength immediately after the market open, answering the core of the user's request for performance over the specific timeframe.

name: Top Positions by Average Opening Hour Return
description: Ranks the open positions by their average first-hour return in descending order, identifying the stocks with the strongest opening momentum.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions had the strongest opening hour performance?' by presenting a ranked list of the best performers.

name: Position Opening Hour Return History
description: Provides a daily time series of the first-hour percentage return for each open position over the last 20 trading days.
usage: Used to analyze the consistency of the opening hour performance, identifying if the strong average is due to a few outlier days or consistent daily gains.

name: Lowest Positions by Average Opening Hour Return
description: Calculates the average percentage price return during the first hour of the trading session (e.g., 9:30 AM to 10:30 AM) over the last 20 trading days for each open position and ranks them in ascending order to identify the securities with the weakest or most negative opening momentum.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions had the weakest opening hour performance?' by identifying the stocks that consistently lose value or perform poorly immediately after the market open.

name: Positions with Negative Average Opening Hour Return
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities where the average percentage price return during the first hour of the trading session over the last 20 trading days is less than zero.
usage: Used when a user wants to isolate all positions that statistically lose money during the first hour, distinguishing them from those that are flat or positive, to identify specific 'weak openers' in the portfolio.

name: Top Positions by Average Closing Hour Return
description: Ranks the open positions by their average percentage return during the last hour of the trading session (e.g., 3:00 PM to 4:00 PM) over the last 20 trading days, listed in descending order.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions had the strongest closing hour performance?' by identifying the stocks with the highest positive average returns in the final hour of trading.

name: Position Average Closing Hour Return
description: Calculates the average percentage price return during the last hour of the trading session (e.g., 3:00 PM to 4:00 PM) over the last 20 trading days for each open position.
usage: Used to retrieve the raw closing hour performance metric for every holding, allowing users to see which stocks are strong or weak into the close without applying a rank sort.

name: Lowest Positions by Average Closing Hour Return
description: Ranks the open positions by their average percentage return during the last hour of the trading session (e.g., 3:00 PM to 4:00 PM) over the last 20 trading days, listed in ascending order.
usage: Used to identify the positions with the weakest or most negative performance during the closing hour, helping to spot stocks that consistently sell off into the close.

name: Position Closing Hour Return History
description: Provides a daily time series of the last-hour percentage return for each open position over the last 20 trading days.
usage: Used to analyze the consistency of closing hour performance, identifying if a strong average is driven by a single outlier day or consistent daily strength late in the session.

name: Positions with Negative Average Closing Hour Return
description: Filters the open positions list to return only those securities where the average percentage price return during the last hour of the trading session over the last 20 trading days is less than zero.
usage: Used when a user wants to isolate all positions that statistically lose money during the closing hour, distinguishing them from those that are flat or positive, to identify specific 'weak closers' in the portfolio.

name: Worst Single Closing Hour Performance Identification
description: Identifies the specific trading day within the last 20 sessions where each position experienced its lowest (most negative) percentage return during the closing hour, and ranks the positions by this worst single-session return.
usage: Used to identify which positions had the most severe single-day sell-offs into the close, distinct from average performance, highlighting the risk of extreme intraday weakness.

name: Position Intraday VWAP Calculation
description: Calculates the Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP) for each open position based on the current trading session's intraday data (price and volume).
usage: Used to determine the average price at which a stock has traded throughout the day, serving as a benchmark to assess if the current close is representing a buying or selling opportunity relative to the day's average value.

name: Top Positions by VWAP to Close Deviation
description: Ranks open positions by the largest absolute difference between the current session's VWAP and the current/close price, identifying the securities with the greatest dislocation from their average traded price.
usage: Used to directly answer which positions are trading furthest away from their volume-weighted average price for the day, highlighting potential exhaustion or momentum extremes.

name: Top Positions by Positive VWAP to Close Deviation
description: Ranks open positions by the largest positive difference between the current session's VWAP and the current/close price (Price > VWAP), highlighting stocks closing strong above the average value.
usage: Used to identify positions experiencing late-session buying pressure or accumulation, where the closing price is significantly higher than the average price paid by volume participants.

name: Top Positions by Negative VWAP to Close Deviation
description: Ranks open positions by the largest negative difference between the current session's VWAP and the current/close price (Price < VWAP), highlighting stocks closing weak below the average value.
usage: Used to identify positions experiencing intraday distribution or selling pressure, where the closing price is significantly lower than the average price paid by volume participants.

name: Position Daily Historical VWAP Series
description: Calculates the Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP) for each open position for every trading day within a specified historical period (e.g., last 30 days).
usage: Used to retrieve the daily VWAP values necessary to perform historical analysis of closing price performance relative to the intraday average price.

name: Position 30-Day Close Above VWAP Frequency
description: Calculates the number of trading days out of the last 30 sessions where the daily closing price exceeded the daily VWAP for each open position.
usage: Used to determine how consistently a position has demonstrated bullish intraday momentum by closing above the average execution price over the last month.

name: Top Positions by 30-Day Close Above VWAP Frequency
description: Ranks open positions by the frequency (count or percentage) of days where the closing price was above VWAP over the last 30 trading sessions.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions most frequently closed above VWAP?' by identifying the stocks with the highest occurrence of bullish closes relative to the volume-weighted average.

name: Position 30-Day Close Below VWAP Frequency
description: Calculates the number of trading days out of the last 30 sessions where the daily closing price was lower than the daily VWAP for each open position.
usage: Used to determine how consistently a position has demonstrated bearish intraday momentum by closing below the average execution price over the last month.

name: Top Positions by 30-Day Close Below VWAP Frequency
description: Ranks open positions by the frequency (count or percentage) of days where the closing price was below VWAP over the last 30 trading sessions.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions most frequently closed below VWAP?' by identifying the stocks with the highest occurrence of bearish closes relative to the volume-weighted average.

name: Position Level 60-Day Realized Skewness
description: Calculates the statistical skewness (third standardized moment) of the daily return distribution for each open position over the last 60 trading days.
usage: Used to assess the asymmetry of returns for specific holdings. Positive skew indicates a higher probability of extreme positive returns (fat right tail), while negative skew indicates a higher risk of large losses (fat left tail).

name: Top Positions by 60-Day Realized Skewness
description: Ranks open positions by their 60-day realized skewness in descending order, identifying the securities with the most positively skewed return distributions.
usage: Used to directly answer which positions have the highest realized skewness, highlighting stocks that have exhibited 'lottery-ticket' like behavior with asymmetric upside potential over the last 3 months.

name: Position Level 60-Day Realized Kurtosis
description: Calculates the statistical kurtosis (fourth standardized moment) of the daily return distribution for each open position over the last 60 trading days.
usage: Used to assess the 'tailedness' of returns for specific holdings. High kurtosis indicates a higher probability of extreme outlier moves (fat tails) relative to a normal distribution, signaling risk of crash or spike events.

name: Top Positions by 60-Day Realized Kurtosis
description: Ranks open positions by their 60-day realized kurtosis in descending order, identifying the securities with the 'fattest' tails or highest probability of extreme returns.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which positions have the highest realized kurtosis?' by identifying the assets most prone to outlier events or 'black swan' risk.

name: Positions Exceeding 60-Day Kurtosis Threshold
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the 60-day realized kurtosis exceeds a user-defined threshold (e.g., > 3.0 for leptokurtic assets).
usage: Used to screen for assets exhibiting extreme outlier behavior without needing a full ranking, specifically answering 'Which positions have fat tails?' or 'Show me positions with kurtosis greater than X'.

name: Position 60-Day Up Day Percentage Calculation
description: Calculates the percentage of trading sessions where the closing price was higher than the previous session's closing price over the last 60 trading days for each open position.
usage: Used to determine the directional reliability or internal strength of a specific holding, answering questions like 'What percent of the last 60 days was this stock up?'

name: Top Positions by 60-Day Up Day Percentage
description: Ranks open positions by their percentage of up days over the last 60 trading days in descending order, highlighting the stocks with the most consistent positive daily price movement.
usage: Used to directly answer which stocks in the portfolio have the most positive daily price momentum and consistency over the last 3 months.

name: Lowest Positions by 60-Day Up Day Percentage
description: Ranks open positions by their lowest percentage of up days over the last 60 trading days, highlighting the stocks with the most frequent negative daily performance.
usage: Used to identify the most unreliable or bearishly trending stocks in the portfolio based on the frequency of down days.

name: Position 60-Day Up/Down Day Count Breakdown
description: Provides the absolute count of up days, down days, and flat days for each open position over the last 60 trading days.
usage: Used to see the raw numbers behind the percentage, distinguishing between a stock that was up 40/60 days versus one that was up 30/60 days.

name: Position 60-Day Down Day Percentage Calculation
description: Calculates the percentage of trading sessions where the closing price was lower than the previous session's closing price over the last 60 trading days for each open position.
usage: Used to determine the specific frequency of negative daily price action for holdings over the last 3 months, complementing the existing Up Day Percentage component.

name: Top Positions by 60-Day Down Day Percentage
description: Ranks open positions by their percentage of down days over the last 60 trading days in descending order, highlighting the securities with the most consistent negative daily price movement.
usage: Used to directly answer which positions had the highest proportion of down days, identifying the weakest daily momentum stocks.

name: Positions Ranked by Correlation to Benchmark
description: Calculates the Pearson correlation coefficient between each open position's daily returns and a specified benchmark ETF (e.g., SPY) over a user-defined lookback period (e.g., 60 trading days), ranking positions from highest to lowest correlation.
usage: Used to directly answer which positions move most closely in sync with the broad market, identifying high beta or high correlation assets that offer little diversification benefit relative to the market.

name: Positions Ranked by Lowest Correlation to Benchmark
description: Ranks open positions by their lowest correlation coefficient relative to a broad market ETF over a specified lookback period, identifying potential hedging assets or diversification opportunities.
usage: Used when a user wants to find positions that are decoupled from the market, which can be useful for reducing systematic risk or finding assets that move independently of the S&P 500.

name: Position vs Benchmark Correlation Breakdown
description: Calculates the realized correlation coefficient for each open position against a specific benchmark ETF over a defined historical period, providing the raw data point without a ranking sort.
usage: Used to retrieve the specific correlation value for every holding relative to the market, enabling manual review or filtering (e.g., identifying all positions with correlation between 0.3 and 0.6).

name: Position vs Benchmark Correlation Significance
description: Calculates the p-values and confidence intervals for the correlation coefficients between open positions and a benchmark index to determine if the relationships are statistically robust or likely due to random chance over the specific period.
usage: Used to validate the reliability of the correlation metric, especially over shorter timeframes (e.g., 60 days), helping to distinguish real market linkage from spurious correlation.

name: Position vs Benchmark Correlation Threshold Filter
description: Filters open positions to return only those where the correlation coefficient with a specified benchmark falls below a user-defined threshold (e.g., < 0.2 or < 0).
usage: Used to strictly screen for assets with non-existent or negative market sensitivity, distinct from a ranked list which includes all values.

name: Positions Ranked by Lowest Absolute Correlation to Benchmark
description: Ranks open positions by the absolute value of their correlation coefficient (|r|) relative to a benchmark, sorting by proximity to zero.
usage: Used to find positions that are truly 'uncorrelated' or statistically independent (idiosyncratic), distinguishing them from negatively correlated assets (hedges) which would appear at the top of a standard correlation sort.

name: Position vs Benchmark Rolling Correlation Series
description: Calculates the rolling correlation coefficient between each open position and a specified benchmark over a defined window (e.g., 20-day) across a historical timeframe.
usage: Used to visualize the trend of market sensitivity over time, determining if the current low correlation is a stable characteristic or a recent divergence.

name: Position vs Benchmark Correlation Stability Analysis
description: Calculates the standard deviation of the correlation coefficient between each position and a benchmark over the lookback period to quantify the volatility of the relationship itself.
usage: Used to assess how reliable the correlation metric is; a low correlation with high standard deviation indicates an unstable relationship that may shift quickly.

name: Positions Ranked by Rolling Beta to Benchmark
description: Calculates the beta coefficient for each open position relative to a specified benchmark ETF (e.g., SPY) using a rolling window (e.g., 60 trading days) and ranks them from highest to lowest.
usage: Used to identify the portfolio holdings with the highest sensitivity to market movements, directly answering which positions are the most volatile relative to the broad market over a specific short-term period.

name: Position Rolling Beta Time Series
description: Calculates the rolling beta coefficient for a specific position against a benchmark over a defined window across a historical timeframe, returning a time series of beta values.
usage: Used to visualize how a position's sensitivity to the market has changed over time, distinguishing it from a single static beta value and helping to identify if a 'high' beta is a stable trait or a recent anomaly.

name: Position Beta Stability Analysis
description: Calculates the statistical stability of the rolling beta for a position over a specified analysis period, including the mean, standard deviation, minimum, and maximum beta values.
usage: Used to assess the reliability of the current beta reading. A high standard deviation indicates that the beta fluctuates significantly, suggesting that the 'highest beta' ranking might change quickly.

name: Positions Ranked by Lowest Rolling Beta to Benchmark
description: Calculates the rolling beta coefficient for each open position relative to a specified benchmark ETF (e.g., SPY) over a defined window (e.g., 60 trading days) and ranks them in ascending order from lowest to highest to identify the least sensitive or inversely correlated assets.
usage: Used when a user asks for the positions with the lowest beta, wants to identify defensive holdings or hedges, or needs to find the stocks with the least market sensitivity over a specific period.

name: Market Sector Performance Ranking
description: Calculates the total percentage return for major market sectors (e.g., Technology, Energy, Financials) typically represented by sector ETFs over a specified timeframe (e.g., 1 month) and ranks them from highest to lowest.
usage: Used to identify which segments of the broader market are leading or lagging in terms of price performance over a specific period, directly answering questions like 'Which sectors are the best performers this month?'.

name: Sector Relative Strength vs Benchmark
description: Calculates the performance difference between major market sectors and a benchmark index (e.g., S&P 500) over a specific timeframe to identify which sectors are outperforming or underperforming the broader market.
usage: Used to determine if a sector's momentum is independent of the market trend, answering questions like 'Which sectors are beating the market this month?' or 'Which sectors are showing true strength relative to the index?'.

name: Sector Technical Momentum Indicators
description: Calculates technical analysis momentum indicators (e.g., RSI, MACD, Moving Average Slope) for major market sector ETFs to gauge the internal strength and sustainability of the current trend.
usage: Used to assess the quality of sector momentum beyond just price return, identifying if a sector is overbought, oversold, or has accelerating trend strength.

name: Market Top Volume Leaders
description: Identifies and ranks the securities with the highest consolidated trading volume for the current session across a specified market index or universe (e.g., S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, Russell 2000).
usage: Used when a user asks for the most active stocks in the broader market, top volume leaders, or wants to identify high-liquidity stocks outside of their personal watchlists or portfolio.

name: Sector Top Volume Leaders
description: Identifies and ranks the securities with the highest consolidated trading volume for the current session within a specific industry sector (e.g., Technology, Energy, Healthcare).
usage: Used when a user asks for the most active stocks in a specific sector or wants to pinpoint where liquidity is concentrated within a particular industry group.

name: Custom Symbol Volume Ranking
description: Takes a user-provided list of ticker symbols (e.g., a specific basket or sector focus list) and ranks them by their current daily trading volume from highest to lowest.
usage: Used for ad-hoc volume comparisons between a specific set of stocks (e.g., 'Rank FAANG stocks by volume') without the requirement of creating a persistent watchlist.

name: Position Level Valuation Ratios
description: Provides key fundamental valuation multiples for each open position, including Price-to-Earnings (P/E), Price-to-Sales (P/S), Price-to-Book (P/B), and Forward P/E.
usage: Used when a user asks for valuation metrics of their holdings, such as 'What is the P/E of my stocks?' or to retrieve the necessary data to calculate portfolio-level valuation averages.

name: Portfolio Weighted Average P/E Ratio
description: Calculates the aggregate Price-to-Earnings ratio for the entire portfolio, weighted by the market value of each position. It handles exclusion of companies with negative earnings (N/A P/E) to prevent calculation errors.
usage: Used to directly answer 'What is the average P/E ratio of my portfolio?' by providing a market-cap weighted view of the portfolio's valuation.

name: Portfolio Median P/E Ratio
description: Calculates the statistical median P/E ratio across all open positions to provide a central tendency measure that is not skewed by extreme outliers or unprofitable companies with negative earnings.
usage: Used when a user wants a more robust representation of portfolio valuation that isn't distorted by one or two extremely high P/E stocks.

name: Sector Average P/E Breakdown
description: Aggregates the weighted average P/E ratio by industry sector, showing how valuation differs across the portfolio's specific industry exposures.
usage: Used to analyze which sectors are driving the portfolio's overall valuation, answering questions like 'Is my Tech exposure making my portfolio expensive?' or comparing valuation across industry groups.

name: Market-Wide Earnings Calendar
description: Retrieves a comprehensive list of publicly traded companies scheduled to report earnings within a specific date range (e.g., 'this week'), typically filtered by a major market universe (e.g., S&P 500, Russell 2000).
usage: Used when a user asks 'Which companies are reporting earnings this week?' for the broad market, independent of their current portfolio or watchlists.

name: Watchlist Earnings Schedule
description: Identifies upcoming earnings dates and times for all symbols contained within a specific user-defined watchlist.
usage: Used when a user wants to know if any stocks they are tracking (but not necessarily owning) are reporting earnings in the near future.

name: Sector Earnings Calendar
description: Filters the market-wide earnings calendar by a specific industry sector (e.g., Technology, Healthcare) for a defined date range.
usage: Used when a user asks for earnings reports within a specific industry segment, such as 'Which tech companies are reporting next week?'

name: Sector Aggregate Correlation to Benchmark
description: Calculates the Pearson correlation coefficient between the weighted daily return series of all positions within a specific sector (e.g., Technology) and a specified benchmark index (e.g., QQQ).
usage: Used to determine how closely a specific sector basket of the portfolio moves in relation to a benchmark, answering questions about the relationship between 'my tech holdings' and QQQ.

name: Sector Rolling Correlation to Benchmark
description: Computes the rolling correlation coefficient between the aggregated sector portfolio returns and the benchmark returns over a specified window (e.g., 20-day) across a historical timeframe.
usage: Used to visualize how the correlation between a specific sector holding and a benchmark has evolved over time, identifying if the relationship is stable or diverging.

name: Sector Aggregate Beta to Benchmark
description: Calculates the beta coefficient for the aggregated portfolio of positions within a specific sector relative to a benchmark, measuring the sector's systematic risk sensitivity.
usage: Used when a user wants to know the volatility or sensitivity of their sector exposure (e.g., Tech) compared to the market (e.g., QQQ), complementing the correlation metric.

name: Sector vs Benchmark Tracking Error
description: Calculates the standard deviation of the difference between the aggregated sector's daily returns and the benchmark's daily returns.
usage: Used to quantify the risk of the sector deviating from the benchmark's performance, providing context on how tightly the sector tracks the index beyond just correlation.

name: Portfolio Dividend Cut Identification
description: Identifies any open positions where the most recently declared dividend per share is lower than the previous declared dividend per share, signaling a payout reduction.
usage: Used to detect negative income events within the user's current holdings, specifically answering 'Did any of my stocks cut their dividend recently?'.

name: Watchlist Dividend Cut Identification
description: Identifies symbols within a specific watchlist where the most recently declared dividend per share is lower than the previous declared dividend per share within the last quarter.
usage: Used to screen a tracked list of stocks for dividend reductions, helping the user identify income risks in stocks they are monitoring but may not own.

name: Market Recent Dividend Cut Screener
description: Screens a predefined market universe (e.g., S&P 500, Russell 2000) for companies that have announced a reduction in their dividend per share within the last quarter.
usage: Used to find broad market examples of dividend cuts or to scan for distressed income stocks across the entire market, not just those currently held or watched.

name: Sector Debt-to-Equity Ratio Ranking
description: Ranks companies within a specific industry sector (e.g., Energy) by their Debt-to-Equity ratio to identify those with the lowest or highest leverage.
usage: Used when a user asks 'Which energy stocks have the best debt-to-equity ratios?' or needs to compare leverage metrics across a specific sector of the market.

name: Symbol Fundamental Debt-to-Equity Ratio
description: Retrieves the Debt-to-Equity ratio and its underlying components (Total Debt and Total Equity) for a specific ticker symbol.
usage: Used when a user asks for the debt-to-equity ratio of a specific company or needs to validate the data used in sector rankings.

name: Symbol Fundamental Leverage Profile
description: Provides a comprehensive view of a company's leverage and solvency using multiple metrics including Debt-to-Equity, Debt-to-Assets, and Interest Coverage Ratio.
usage: Used to assess the overall debt health of a specific company beyond a single ratio, determining if 'best' implies low leverage or high ability to service debt.

name: Historical Debt-to-Equity Trend
description: Calculates the Debt-to-Equity ratio for a specific ticker over multiple historical periods (e.g., quarterly for the last 5 years) to show the trend of leverage.
usage: Used to determine if a company's debt profile is improving or deteriorating over time, adding context to the current 'best' ranking.

name: Interest Rate Sensitive Sector Exposure
description: Aggregates the portfolio's market value by sectors historically identified as sensitive to interest rate changes (e.g., Real Estate, Utilities, Financials, Telecommunications).
usage: Used when a user asks for their exposure to interest rate sensitive industries, allowing for a quick view of capital concentration in sectors that typically react significantly to monetary policy changes.

name: Interest Rate Sensitive Holdings Breakdown
description: Provides a detailed list of individual open positions that fall within interest rate sensitive industry classifications, displaying their specific market value and weight within the rate-sensitive bucket.
usage: Used to identify exactly which specific stocks or assets are driving the portfolio's interest rate sensitivity, answering questions like 'Which of my stocks are rate sensitive?'

name: Portfolio Interest Rate Duration (Sensitivity)
description: Calculates the aggregate interest rate duration or sensitivity score for the equity portfolio, often expressed as the estimated percentage change in portfolio value for a 1% (100 basis point) shift in interest rates, based on the historical duration characteristics of the underlying sectors (e.g., REITs and Utilities).
usage: Used to quantify the risk exposure to interest rate movements, answering questions about how much the portfolio might gain or lose if rates rise or fall, distinct from just the sector allocation.

name: Symbol REIT FFO & Payout Ratio Metrics
description: Retrieves REIT-specific fundamental data including Funds From Operations (FFO) and Adjusted Funds From Operations (AFFO) to calculate the dividend payout ratio, which is the primary measure of dividend sustainability for Real Estate Investment Trusts.
usage: Used when analyzing a specific REIT ticker to determine if its dividend is safe from being cut, or to provide the raw data (FFO, AFFO, Payout Ratio) needed for ranking.

name: Market REITs Ranked by Dividend Coverage
description: Screens the market universe of Real Estate Investment Trusts and ranks them by their dividend payout ratio (lowest to highest) to identify the companies with the most sustainable dividend coverage relative to their Funds From Operations.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Which REITs have the most sustainable dividends?' by providing a ranked list of the safest payers based on coverage ratios.

name: REIT FFO & Dividend Growth Trend
description: Tracks the historical trend of Funds From Operations (FFO) and Dividends Per Share over multiple quarters or years to assess if a REIT is growing its cash flow fast enough to sustain or increase dividend payments.
usage: Used to analyze the trajectory of dividend sustainability over time, determining if coverage is improving (FFO growing faster than dividends) or deteriorating.

name: Market New 52-Week Highs Screener
description: Scans a predefined market universe (e.g., S&P 500, Russell 2000) to identify stocks where the current session's high price exceeds the previous 52-week high.
usage: Used when a user asks for stocks hitting new yearly highs, market leaders, or breadth analysis across the entire market.

name: Sector New 52-Week Highs Breakdown
description: Aggregates the list of stocks hitting new 52-week highs by their industry sector, providing counts and representative tickers for each group.
usage: Used to identify which sectors are driving the market's strength or showing the most momentum via new highs.

name: Watchlist New 52-Week Highs Identification
description: Scans a specific user-defined watchlist to identify symbols that have exceeded their 52-week high during the current session.
usage: Used when a user wants to know if any stocks they are tracking (but not necessarily owning) are hitting new highs.

name: Portfolio New 52-Week High Breakout Events
description: Identifies open positions where today's intraday high exceeded the 52-week high recorded at the previous session's close, confirming a new breakout.
usage: Used to highlight which current holdings are achieving all-time highs or 52-week peaks, distinct from simply being 'near' a high.

name: Top Positions by Long-Term Fundamental Beta
description: Ranks open positions by their beta coefficient calculated over a long-term historical period (typically 3 or 5 years) rather than a short-term rolling window.
usage: Used when a user wants to identify holdings with the highest long-term systematic risk relative to the market, distinct from the short-term 60-day rolling beta currently in the library.

name: Top Positions by Unlevered Beta
description: Calculates the unlevered beta (asset beta) for each position by removing the financial leverage effect (debt/equity) and ranks them from highest to lowest.
usage: Used to compare the underlying business risk of holdings without the distortion of different capital structures, answering 'Which of my business assets are most volatile?'

name: Top Positions by Sector Relative Beta
description: Calculates the beta coefficient for each position relative to its specific sector ETF (e.g., XLK for Tech) rather than the broad market benchmark, and ranks them from highest to lowest.
usage: Used to identify which holdings have the highest sensitivity to their specific industry peers, answering 'Which stock is most volatile within its sector?'

name: Recent News Headlines for Symbol
description: Retrieves a list of the most recent news headlines, summaries, and metadata for a specified ticker symbol to provide context for the current news cycle.
usage: Used when a user asks for the latest news, recent headlines, or needs the source material to understand what is driving price action for a specific stock.

name: News Sentiment Score for Symbol
description: Calculates an aggregated sentiment score (typically ranging from -1 to 1 or 0-100) for a specific ticker based on Natural Language Processing (NLP) analysis of recent news articles.
usage: Used when a user asks for the sentiment of the news, how positive or negative the current news flow is, or the general market mood regarding a specific stock.

name: News Sentiment Distribution Breakdown
description: Categorizes recent news articles for a specific ticker into sentiment buckets (Positive, Negative, Neutral) and provides the count and percentage for each to show the balance of coverage.
usage: Used to understand the composition of news flow, determining if the news is predominantly bullish, bearish, or mixed over a specific period.

name: Market ETF Expense Ratio Ranking
description: Screens the entire market universe of Exchange Traded Funds, filters them by a specific asset class or investment category (e.g., Emerging Markets), and ranks them by their annual expense ratio from lowest to highest.
usage: Used when a user asks for the cheapest ETFs in a specific sector or asset class, specifically identifying funds with the lowest management fees.

name: Symbol ETF Fundamentals
description: Retrieves the fundamental profile data for a specific ETF ticker, including its expense ratio, assets under management (AUM), tracking index, and dividend yield.
usage: Used when a user asks for the specific expense ratio or management fee of a particular ETF, or needs to verify the size and benchmark of a fund.

name: Position Investment Style Classification
description: Identifies the investment style (Value, Core, Growth) for each open position based on underlying factor scores (e.g., P/E, P/B, dividend yield) provided by a data provider.
usage: Used to categorize portfolio holdings into style buckets, specifically identifying which stocks qualify as 'Value' stocks to enable filtering and analysis.

name: Portfolio Weighted Average Price-to-Book Ratio
description: Calculates the aggregate Price-to-Book ratio for the entire portfolio, weighted by the market value of each position.
usage: Used to determine the overall valuation of the portfolio using the P/B metric, or to compare the portfolio's book-value valuation against a benchmark.

name: Value Stocks Weighted Average P/B Ratio
description: Calculates the weighted average Price-to-Book ratio specifically for the subset of open positions classified as 'Value' stocks.
usage: Used to directly answer questions regarding the valuation of the value-oriented portion of the portfolio, such as 'What is the P/B ratio of my value stocks?'

name: Sector Stocks Below 200-Day Moving Average List
description: Scans a specified industry sector (e.g., Technology) and returns a list of constituents that are currently trading below their 200-day simple moving average.
usage: Used when a user asks for a list of stocks in a specific sector that are in a long-term downtrend or trading below a key support level (e.g., 'Which tech stocks are below their 200-day MA?').

name: Sector 200-Day Moving Average Breadth
description: Calculates the percentage and count of stocks within a specific industry sector that are currently trading below their 200-day moving average to gauge overall sector health and trend weakness.
usage: Used to determine the extent of the downturn in a sector (e.g., 'Is the whole tech sector crashing or just a few names?'), providing context to the list of underperforming stocks.

name: Sector Constituent Moving Average Status Breakdown
description: Returns a comprehensive list of all constituents in a specified sector along with their current status relative to the 200-day moving average (Above or Below), allowing for full market analysis without pre-filtering.
usage: Used when a user wants to see the complete picture of a sector's technical positioning, identifying both the laggards (below MA) and the leaders (above MA) in a single view.

name: Sector Earnings Growth Consistency Ranking
description: Identifies and ranks stocks within a specific sector (e.g., Consumer Staples) based on the stability of their historical earnings per share (EPS) growth. Consistency is measured by the standard deviation of the year-over-year growth rate or the R-squared value of the EPS trend line over a multi-year period.
usage: Used to directly answer questions like 'Which consumer staples stocks have the most consistent earnings growth?' by filtering a sector for companies with steady, predictable profit expansion.

name: Symbol Historical EPS Trend Analysis
description: Retrieves the historical earnings per share (EPS) data points for a specific security over a defined period and calculates the linear regression trend line, compound annual growth rate (CAGR), and R-squared (goodness of fit) to quantify the consistency of the growth.
usage: Used to verify the consistency of earnings growth for a specific stock identified in the ranking, allowing the user to visualize the trend line and see if growth has been steady or erratic.

name: Earnings Growth Volatility Calculation
description: Calculates the statistical volatility (standard deviation or coefficient of variation) of the earnings growth rate for a specific security or a list of securities over a specified historical timeframe.
usage: Used to quantify the 'consistency' of earnings numerically, where a lower value indicates more stable and predictable growth compared to the average growth rate.

name: Benchmark Sharpe Ratio
description: Calculates the annualized Sharpe ratio for a specified benchmark index (e.g., SPY) over a defined timeframe using daily total returns and a risk-free rate.
usage: Used when a user asks for the Sharpe ratio of the S&P 500, the risk-adjusted return of the market, or needs to compare their portfolio's efficiency against the benchmark's efficiency.

name: Portfolio vs Benchmark Sharpe Comparison
description: Calculates the Sharpe ratio for both the portfolio and the benchmark side-by-side and determines the difference (Excess Sharpe) to show which entity achieved better risk-adjusted performance.
usage: Used to directly answer 'Did my portfolio beat the S&P 500 on a risk-adjusted basis?' or 'What is my Sharpe ratio relative to the market?'

name: Rolling Benchmark Sharpe Ratio Series
description: Calculates the annualized Sharpe ratio for a specified benchmark index using a rolling window (e.g., 20-day) across a historical timeframe.
usage: Used to visualize how the benchmark's risk-adjusted performance has trended over time, enabling a dynamic comparison against the portfolio's rolling Sharpe ratio.

name: Sector Total Cash Position Ranking
description: Ranks companies within a specific industry sector (e.g., Biotechnology) by their total cash and cash equivalents on the balance sheet.
usage: Used to identify which companies in a specific sector hold the largest absolute amount of cash, directly answering questions about 'strongest cash positions' in terms of total liquidity.

name: Sector Net Cash Position Ranking
description: Ranks companies within a specific sector by their net cash position (Total Cash minus Total Debt), highlighting companies with cash-rich, debt-free balance sheets.
usage: Used to identify the most financially secure companies in a sector that have more cash than debt, distinguishing 'strong' positions from those with high gross cash but also high leverage.

name: Sector Current Ratio Ranking
description: Ranks companies within a specific sector by their current ratio (Current Assets divided by Current Liabilities) to assess short-term liquidity strength.
usage: Used when 'strong cash position' implies a strong ability to pay short-term obligations, providing a more comprehensive liquidity view than cash alone.

name: Symbol Correlation with 10-Year Treasury Yield
description: Calculates the correlation coefficient between a specific stock's historical daily returns and the daily percentage change in the 10-Year US Treasury Yield over a specified lookback period.
usage: Used to identify which specific stocks have historically moved in tandem with rising interest rates. A high positive correlation suggests the stock benefits from or is sensitive to rising yields, whereas a negative correlation suggests it behaves like a bond proxy.

name: Bank Net Interest Margin (NIM) Breakdown
description: Provides the current Net Interest Margin (NIM) percentage for banking and financial institutions, along with historical trend data (e.g., quarterly NIM over the last 5 years) to show expansion or contraction.
usage: Used to determine which banks are currently most profitable from their lending spreads. Rising rates generally benefit banks with high or expanding NIMs, making this a key fundamental metric for identifying the primary beneficiaries of a rate hike cycle.

name: Symbol Sensitivity to Yield Curve Spread (2s10s)
description: Calculates the beta or correlation of a stock's returns against changes in the yield curve spread (the difference between the 10-Year and 2-Year Treasury yields).
